contentBlocks.original:
श्रीललिताम्बिकायै नमः ।
देव्युवाच ।
देवदेव महादेव सच्चिदानन्दविग्रहा ।
सुन्दर्याहृदयं स्तोत्रं परं कौतूहलं विभो ॥ १॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
athaśrīlalitāhṛdayastōtram ॥
śrīlalitāmbikāyai namaḥ ।
dēvyuvācha ।
dēvadēva mahādēva sachchidānandavigrahā ।
sundaryāhṛdayaṃ stōtraṃ paraṃ kautūhalaṃ vibhō ॥ 1॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to Sri Lalitambika. The Goddess (Parvati) spoke: "O God of gods, O Mahadeva, O embodiment of Sat-Chit-Ananda (existence-consciousness-bliss), I am most curious, O Lord, about the Hridayam (heart-hymn) of the Sundari (Lalitha). (1)"

contentBlocks.original:
साधु साधुत्वया प्राज्ञे लोकानुग्रहकारकम् ।
रहस्यमपिवक्ष्यामि सावधानमनाःश‍ऋणु ॥ २॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īśvarauvācha ।
sādhu sādhutvayā prājñē lōkānugrahakārakam ।
rahasyamapivakṣyāmi sāvadhānamanāḥśa‍ṛṇu ॥ 2॥
contentBlocks.translation: "Well asked, well asked, O wise one — this is for the benefit of all the world. I shall reveal this secret as well — listen with an attentive mind. (2)"

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीविद्यां जगतां धात्रीं सर्ग्गस्थितिलयेश्वरीम् ।
नमामिललितां नित्यां भक्तानामिष्टदायिनीम् ॥ ३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīvidyāṃ jagatāṃ dhātrīṃ sarggasthitilayēśvarīm ।
namāmilalitāṃ nityāṃ bhaktānāmiṣṭadāyinīm ॥ 3॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Lalitha, who is the Sri Vidya herself, the sustainer of all the worlds, the sovereign of creation, preservation and dissolution, the eternal one, the bestower of all desires on devotees. (3)

contentBlocks.original:
बिन्दुत्रिकोणसम्युक्तं वसुकोणसमन्वितम् ।
दशकोणद्वयोपेतं चतुर्द्दश समन्वितम् ॥ ४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bindutrikōṇasamyuktaṃ vasukōṇasamanvitam ।
daśakōṇadvayōpētaṃ chaturddaśa samanvitam ॥ 4॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Sri Chakra — endowed with the bindu and the triangle, the eight-angle (vasukona), the two ten-angle figures, and the fourteen-angle figure. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
दलाष्टकेसरोपेतं दलषोडशकान्वितम् ।
वृत्तत्रययान्वितम्भूमिसदनत्रयभूषितम् ॥ ५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dalāṣṭakēsarōpētaṃ dalaṣōḍaśakānvitam ।
vṛttatrayayānvitambhūmisadanatrayabhūṣitam ॥ 5॥
contentBlocks.translation: — adorned with the eight-petal ring, the sixteen-petal ring, the three circles, and beautified by the three enclosing squares. (5)

contentBlocks.original:
नमामि ललिताचक्रं भक्तानामिष्टदायकम् ।
अमृताम्भोनिधिन्तत्र रत्नद्वीपं नमाम्यहम् ॥ ६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namāmi lalitāchakraṃ bhaktānāmiṣṭadāyakam ।
amṛtāmbhōnidhintatra ratnadvīpaṃ namāmyaham ॥ 6॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Lalitha Chakra — the giver of all desires to devotees. I bow to the ocean of nectar therein, and I bow to the Island of Jewels (Ratna Dvipa) within it. (6)

contentBlocks.original:
नानावृक्षमहोद्यानं वन्देहं कल्पवाटिकाम् ।
सन्तानवाटिकांवन्दे हरिचन्दनवाटिकाम् ॥ ७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nānāvṛkṣamahōdyānaṃ vandēhaṃ kalpavāṭikām ।
santānavāṭikāṃvandē harichandanavāṭikām ॥ 7॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the great garden of manifold trees therein; I bow to the grove of Kalpa trees. I bow to the Santana grove and the Harichandan grove. (7)

contentBlocks.original:
मन्दारवाटिकां पारिजातवाटीं मुदा भजे ।
नमामितव देवेशि कदम्बवनवाटिकाम् ॥ ८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mandāravāṭikāṃ pārijātavāṭīṃ mudā bhajē ।
namāmitava dēvēśi kadambavanavāṭikām ॥ 8॥
contentBlocks.translation: With joy I worship the Mandara grove and the Parijata grove. O Goddess, I bow to your grove of Kadamba trees. (8)

contentBlocks.original:
पुष्यरागमहारत्नप्राकारं प्रणमाम्यहम् ।
पद्मरागादिमणिभिःप्राकारं सर्वदा भजे ॥ ९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
puṣyarāgamahāratnaprākāraṃ praṇamāmyaham ।
padmarāgādimaṇibhiḥprākāraṃ sarvadā bhajē ॥ 9॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the great enclosure-wall of Pushyaraga jewels. I always bow to the enclosure of Padmaraga and other gems. (9)

contentBlocks.original:
गोमेदरत्नप्राकारं वज्रप्राकारमाश्रये ।
वैडूर्यरत्नप्राकारम्प्रणमामि कुलेश्वरी ॥ १०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gōmēdaratnaprākāraṃ vajraprākāramāśrayē ।
vaiḍūryaratnaprākārampraṇamāmi kulēśvarī ॥ 10॥
contentBlocks.translation: I take refuge in the enclosure of Gomeda gems and the enclosure of Vajra (diamond). O Kuleshvari, I bow to the enclosure of Vaidurya gems. (10)

contentBlocks.original:
इन्द्रनीलाख्यरत्नानां प्राकारं प्रणमाम्यहम् ।
मुक्ताफलमहारत्नप्राकारम्प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ ११॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
indranīlākhyaratnānāṃ prākāraṃ praṇamāmyaham ।
muktāphalamahāratnaprākārampraṇamāmyaham ॥ 11॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the enclosure of Indranila (sapphire) gems. I bow to the great enclosure of Muktaphala (pearls). (11)

contentBlocks.original:
मरताख्यमहारत्नप्राकाराय नमोनमः ।
विद्रुमाख्यमहारत्नप्राकारम्प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ १२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
maratākhyamahāratnaprākārāya namōnamaḥ ।
vidrumākhyamahāratnaprākārampraṇamāmyaham ॥ 12॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations again and again to the enclosure of great Marata (emerald) jewels. I bow to the enclosure of great Vidruma (coral) jewels. (12)

contentBlocks.original:
माणिक्यमण्डपं रत्नसहस्रस्तम्भमण्डपम् ।
ललिते!तवदेवेशि भजाम्यमृतवापिकाम् ॥ १३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
māṇikyamaṇḍapaṃ ratnasahasrastambhamaṇḍapam ।
lalitē!tavadēvēśi bhajāmyamṛtavāpikām ॥ 13॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lalitha, O Devi, O Goddess — I bow to the ruby-pillared hall and the hall of a thousand jewelled pillars; I worship your Amrita Vapika (lake of nectar). (13)

contentBlocks.original:
आनन्दवापिकां वन्देविमर्शवापिकां भजे ।
भजेबालातपोल्गारं चन्द्रिकोगारिकां भजे ॥ १४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ānandavāpikāṃ vandēvimarśavāpikāṃ bhajē ।
bhajēbālātapōlgāraṃ chandrikōgārikāṃ bhajē ॥ 14॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Ananda Vapika (lake of bliss); I worship the Vimarsha Vapika (lake of reflection). I worship the warmth-garden of Bala (the Goddess as a young girl) and the Chandrika garden (moonlight grove). (14)

contentBlocks.original:
महाश‍ऋङ्गारपरिखां महापत्माटवीं भजे ।
चिन्तामणिमहारत्नगृहराजं नमाम्यहम् ॥ १५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāśa‍ṛṅgāraparikhāṃ mahāpatmāṭavīṃ bhajē ।
chintāmaṇimahāratnagṛharājaṃ namāmyaham ॥ 15॥
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the great moat of Mahashringara (great love) and the great Patma forest. I bow to the great Chintamani gem palace, the palace of palaces. (15)

contentBlocks.original:
पूर्वान्नायमयं पूर्व्वद्वारं देवि नमाम्यहम् ।
दक्षिणान्नायरूपन्तेदक्षिणद्वारमाश्रये ॥ १६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pūrvānnāyamayaṃ pūrvvadvāraṃ dēvi namāmyaham ।
dakṣiṇānnāyarūpantēdakṣiṇadvāramāśrayē ॥ 16॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, I bow to your Eastern Gate, which is the form of the Eastern Amnaya. I take refuge in your Southern Gate, which is the form of the Southern Amnaya. (16)

contentBlocks.original:
नमामि पश्चिमद्वारं पश्चिमाम्नाय रूपकम् ।
वन्देहमुत्तरद्वारमुत्तराम्नायरूपकम् ॥ १७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namāmi paśchimadvāraṃ paśchimāmnāya rūpakam ।
vandēhamuttaradvāramuttarāmnāyarūpakam ॥ 17॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Western Gate, which is the form of the Western Amnaya. I bow to the Northern Gate, which is the form of the Northern Amnaya. (17)

contentBlocks.original:
ऊर्द्ध्वाम्नायमयं वन्दे ह्यूर्द्धद्वारं कुलेश्वरि ।
ललितेतव देवेशि महासिंहासनं भजे ॥ १८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ūrddhvāmnāyamayaṃ vandē hyūrddhadvāraṃ kulēśvari ।
lalitētava dēvēśi mahāsiṃhāsanaṃ bhajē ॥ 18॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Upward Gate, which is the form of the Upper Amnaya, O Kuleshvari. O Lalitha, O Devi, O Goddess — I worship your great throne. (18)

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मात्मकं मञ्चपादमेकं तव नमाम्यहम् ।
एकंविष्णुमयं मञ्चपादमन्यं नमाम्यहम् ॥ १९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmātmakaṃ mañchapādamēkaṃ tava namāmyaham ।
ēkaṃviṣṇumayaṃ mañchapādamanyaṃ namāmyaham ॥ 19॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to one leg of your throne, which is of the nature of Brahma. I bow to another leg of your throne, which is of the nature of Vishnu. (19)

contentBlocks.original:
एकं रुद्रमयं मञ्चपादमन्यं नमाम्यहम् ।
मञ्चपादम्ममाम्येकं तव देवीश्वरात्मकम् ॥ २०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ēkaṃ rudramayaṃ mañchapādamanyaṃ namāmyaham ।
mañchapādammamāmyēkaṃ tava dēvīśvarātmakam ॥ 20॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to another leg of your throne, which is of the nature of Rudra. I bow to the leg of your throne, O Goddess, which is of the nature of Ishvara. (20)

contentBlocks.original:
मञ्चैकफलकं वन्दे सदाशिवमयं शुभम् ।
नमामितेहंसतूलतल्पकं परमेश्वरी! ॥ २१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mañchaikaphalakaṃ vandē sadāśivamayaṃ śubham ।
namāmitēhaṃsatūlatalpakaṃ paramēśvarī! ॥ 21॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the auspicious single plank of your throne, which is of the nature of Sadashiva. O supreme Goddess, I bow to the swan-feather mattress of your seat. (21)

contentBlocks.original:
नमामिते हंसतूलमहोपाधानमुत्तमम् ।
कौस्तुभास्तरणंवन्दे तव नित्यं कुलेश्वरी ॥ २२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namāmitē haṃsatūlamahōpādhānamuttamam ।
kaustubhāstaraṇaṃvandē tava nityaṃ kulēśvarī ॥ 22॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the great swan-feather pillow of your couch. O Kuleshvari, I bow always to the Kaustubha gem spread as a sheet on your seat. (22)

contentBlocks.original:
महावितानिकां वन्दे महायविनिकां भजे ।
एवं पूजागृहं ध्यात्वा श्रीचक्रे श्रीशिवां भजे ॥ २३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāvitānikāṃ vandē mahāyavinikāṃ bhajē ।
ēvaṃ pūjāgṛhaṃ dhyātvā śrīchakrē śrīśivāṃ bhajē ॥ 23॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the great canopy; I worship the great curtain. Having thus meditated on the puja chamber, I worship the auspicious Lalitha in the Sri Chakra. (23)

contentBlocks.original:
स्वदक्षिणे स्थापयामि भागे पुष्पाक्षतादिकान् ।
अमितांस्तेमहादेवि दीपान् सन्दर्शयाम्यहम् ॥ २४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
svadakṣiṇē sthāpayāmi bhāgē puṣpākṣatādikān ।
amitāṃstēmahādēvi dīpān sandarśayāmyaham ॥ 24॥
contentBlocks.translation: I place flowers and aksharas (grains) to your right, O great Goddess. I present to you countless lamps, O Mahadevi. (24)

contentBlocks.original:
मूलेन त्रिपुराचक्रं तव सम्पूज्ययाम्यहम् ।
त्रिभिःखण्डैस्तवख्यातैः पूजयामि महेश्वरि! ॥ २५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mūlēna tripurāchakraṃ tava sampūjyayāmyaham ।
tribhiḥkhaṇḍaistavakhyātaiḥ pūjayāmi mahēśvari! ॥ 25॥
contentBlocks.translation: With the root mantra I worship your Tripura Chakra. O Maheshvari, I worship you with the three sections of your Tripura Vidya. (25)

contentBlocks.original:
वाय्वग्नि जलसम्युक्तं प्राणायामैरहं शिवै ।
शोषाणान्दाहनं चैव करोमि प्लावनं तथा ॥ २६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vāyvagni jalasamyuktaṃ prāṇāyāmairahaṃ śivai ।
śōṣāṇāndāhanaṃ chaiva karōmi plāvanaṃ tathā ॥ 26॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Shiva, with pranayama — united with air, fire and water — I perform the drying (shosha), the burning (dahana) and the flooding (plavana) of the body. (26)

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिवारं मूलमन्त्रेण प्राणायामं करोम्यहम् ।
पाषण्डकारिणोभूता भूमौये चान्तरिक्षके ॥ २७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trivāraṃ mūlamantrēṇa prāṇāyāmaṃ karōmyaham ।
pāṣaṇḍakāriṇōbhūtā bhūmauyē chāntarikṣakē ॥ 27॥
contentBlocks.translation: With the root mantra I perform pranayama three times. Whatever evil spirits and beings stand on earth and in the atmosphere — (27)

contentBlocks.original:
करोम्यनेन मन्त्रेण तालत्रयमहं शिवे ।
नारायणोऽहम्ब्रह्माहं भैरवोऽहं शिवोस्म्यहम् ॥ २८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karōmyanēna mantrēṇa tālatrayamahaṃ śivē ।
nārāyaṇō'hambrahmāhaṃ bhairavō'haṃ śivōsmyaham ॥ 28॥
contentBlocks.translation: I clap three times with this mantra, O Shiva. "I am Narayana, I am Brahma, I am Bhairava, I am Shiva." (28)

contentBlocks.original:
देवोहं परमानन्दोऽस्म्यहं त्रिपुरसुन्दरि ।
ध्यात्वावै वज्रकवचं न्यासं तव करोम्यहम् ॥ २९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvōhaṃ paramānandō'smyahaṃ tripurasundari ।
dhyātvāvai vajrakavachaṃ nyāsaṃ tava karōmyaham ॥ 29॥
contentBlocks.translation: "I am the Deva, I am supreme bliss, O Tripurasundari." Having thus meditated on the Vajra armour, I perform nyasa (mantra-placement) in your form, O Goddess. (29)

contentBlocks.original:
कुमारीबीजसम्युक्तं महात्रिपुरसुन्दरि! ।
मांरक्षरक्षेति हृदि करोम्यज्ञलिमीश्वरि! ॥ ३०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kumārībījasamyuktaṃ mahātripurasundari! ।
māṃrakṣarakṣēti hṛdi karōmyajñalimīśvari! ॥ 30॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Mahatripurasundari, united with the seed-mantra of the Kumari Goddess — "protect me, protect me" — thus I perform the anjali (folded-hands salutation) in my heart, O Goddess. (30)

contentBlocks.original:
महादेव्यासनायेति प्रकरोम्यासनं शिवे ।
चक्रासनन्नमस्यामि सर्वमन्त्रासनं शिवे ॥ ३१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahādēvyāsanāyēti prakarōmyāsanaṃ śivē ।
chakrāsanannamasyāmi sarvamantrāsanaṃ śivē ॥ 31॥
contentBlocks.translation: I prepare the seat for the great Goddess with the mantra "Mahadevyasanaya." I bow to the seat of the Sri Chakra and to the seat of all mantras, O Shiva. (31)

contentBlocks.original:
साद्ध्यसिद्धासनं मन्त्रैरेभिर्युक्तं महेश्वरि ।
करोम्यस्मिञ्चक्रमन्त्रैर्देवतासनमुत्तमम् ॥ ३२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sāddhyasiddhāsanaṃ mantrairēbhiryuktaṃ mahēśvari ।
karōmyasmiñchakramantrairdēvatāsanamuttamam ॥ 32॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Maheshvari, with the siddhasana mantra — I prepare for this Chakra the supreme seat of all the deities united with mantras. (32)

contentBlocks.original:
करोम्यथ षडङ्गाख्यं मातृकां च कलां न्यसे ।
श्रीकण्टङ्केशवं चैव प्रपञ्चं योगमातृकाम् ॥ ३३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karōmyatha ṣaḍaṅgākhyaṃ mātṛkāṃ cha kalāṃ nyasē ।
śrīkaṇṭaṅkēśavaṃ chaiva prapañchaṃ yōgamātṛkām ॥ 33॥
contentBlocks.translation: I perform the shadanga (six-limbed) nyasa. I place the Matrika (the divine mother-alphabet) and the Kala (divine art-rays). I place Shrikantha, Keshava, the Prapancha mantra and the Yoga Matrika. (33)

contentBlocks.original:
तत्त्वन्यासं ततः कूर्व्वे चतुष्पीटं यथाचरे ।
लघुषोढान्ततः कूर्व्वे शक्तिन्यासं महोत्तमम् ॥ ३४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tattvanyāsaṃ tataḥ kūrvvē chatuṣpīṭaṃ yathācharē ।
laghuṣōḍhāntataḥ kūrvvē śaktinyāsaṃ mahōttamam ॥ 34॥
contentBlocks.translation: I then perform Tattva Nyasa and the Chatushpitha as practised in tradition. I then perform the Laghu Shodha and the supremely excellent Shakti Nyasa. (34)

contentBlocks.original:
पीटन्यासं ततः कुर्वे देवतावाहनं प्रिये ।
कुङ्कुमन्यासकञ्चैव चक्रन्यासमथाचरे ॥ ३५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pīṭanyāsaṃ tataḥ kurvē dēvatāvāhanaṃ priyē ।
kuṅkumanyāsakañchaiva chakranyāsamathācharē ॥ 35॥
contentBlocks.translation: I then perform the Pitha Nyasa and the invocation of the deity, O beloved. I practice the Kunkuma Nyasa and then the Chakra Nyasa. (35)

contentBlocks.original:
चक्रन्यासं ततः कुर्व्वे न्यासं कामकलाद्वयम् ।
षोडशार्ण्णमहामन्त्रैरङ्गन्यासङ्करोम्यहम् ॥ ३६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chakranyāsaṃ tataḥ kurvvē nyāsaṃ kāmakalādvayam ।
ṣōḍaśārṇṇamahāmantrairaṅganyāsaṅkarōmyaham ॥ 36॥
contentBlocks.translation: I then perform the Chakra Nyasa and the Kama Kala Dvaya nyasa. I perform the Anga Nyasa with the sixteen-syllabled Mahamantra. (36)

contentBlocks.original:
महाषोढां ततः कुर्व्वे शाम्भवं च महाप्रिये ।
ततोमूलम्प्रजप्त्वाथ पादुकाञ्च ततः परम् ॥ ३७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāṣōḍhāṃ tataḥ kurvvē śāmbhavaṃ cha mahāpriyē ।
tatōmūlamprajaptvātha pādukāñcha tataḥ param ॥ 37॥
contentBlocks.translation: I then perform the Mahashodha and then the Shambhava ritual, O greatly beloved. Then, having recited the root mantra and then the sandals (paduka), — (37)

contentBlocks.original:
गुरवे सम्यगर्च्याथ देवतां हृदिसम्भजे ।
करोमिमण्डलं वृत्तं चतुरश्रं शिवप्रिये ॥ ३८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
guravē samyagarchyātha dēvatāṃ hṛdisambhajē ।
karōmimaṇḍalaṃ vṛttaṃ chaturaśraṃ śivapriyē ॥ 38॥
contentBlocks.translation: — having duly worshipped the Guru, I then inwardly worship the Goddess. I prepare the round mandala and the square mandala, O Shivapriya. (38)

contentBlocks.original:
पुष्पैरभ्यर्च्च्यसाधारं शङ्खं सम्पूजयामहम् ।
अर्च्चयामिषडङ्गेन जलमापूरयाम्यहम् ॥ ३९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
puṣpairabhyarchchyasādhāraṃ śaṅkhaṃ sampūjayāmaham ।
archchayāmiṣaḍaṅgēna jalamāpūrayāmyaham ॥ 39॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having worshipped with flowers the sadhara (with support), I worship the conch. I worship with the six limbs and fill the conch with water. (39)

contentBlocks.original:
ददामि चादिमं बिन्दुं कुर्वे मूलाभिमन्त्रितम् ।
तज्जलेनजगन्मातस्त्रिकोणं वृत्तसम्युतम् ॥ ४०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dadāmi chādimaṃ binduṃ kurvē mūlābhimantritam ।
tajjalēnajaganmātastrikōṇaṃ vṛttasamyutam ॥ 40॥
contentBlocks.translation: I offer the first dot (bindu) and sanctify the root mantra. O Mother of the universe, I sprinkle the triangle joined with the circle with that water. (40)

contentBlocks.original:
षल्कोणं चतुरश्रञ्च मण्डलं प्रणमाम्यहम् ।
विद्ययापूजयामीह त्रिखण्डेन तु पूजनम् ॥ ४१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ṣalkōṇaṃ chaturaśrañcha maṇḍalaṃ praṇamāmyaham ।
vidyayāpūjayāmīha trikhaṇḍēna tu pūjanam ॥ 41॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the mandala consisting of hexagon and square. I worship here the three-sectioned Vidya and the Trisection worship. (41)

contentBlocks.original:
बीजेनवृत्तषल्कोणं पूजयामि तवप्रिये ।
तस्मिन्देवीकलात्मानां मणिमण्डलमाश्रये ॥ ४२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bījēnavṛttaṣalkōṇaṃ pūjayāmi tavapriyē ।
tasmindēvīkalātmānāṃ maṇimaṇḍalamāśrayē ॥ 42॥
contentBlocks.translation: O beloved, I worship the circle and hexagon with the seed mantra. Within that, I take refuge in the maṇimaṇḍala (jewel-circle) of the Devi Kala. (42)

contentBlocks.original:
धूम्रार्च्चिषं नमस्यामि ऊष्मां च ज्वलनीं तथा ।
ज्वालिनीञ्च नमस्यामि वन्देहं विस्पुलिङ्गिनीम् ॥ ४३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhūmrārchchiṣaṃ namasyāmi ūṣmāṃ cha jvalanīṃ tathā ।
jvālinīñcha namasyāmi vandēhaṃ vispuliṅginīm ॥ 43॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Dhumrarchis, Ushma, Jvalini, and I bow to Visphulingini (the sparkling one). (43)

contentBlocks.original:
सुश्रियं च सुरूपाञ्चकम्पिलां प्रणमाम्यहम् ।
नौमिहव्यवहां नित्यां भजे कव्यवहां कलाम् ॥ ४४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suśriyaṃ cha surūpāñchakampilāṃ praṇamāmyaham ।
naumihavyavahāṃ nityāṃ bhajē kavyavahāṃ kalām ॥ 44॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Sushri and Surupa; I bow to Kampila. I bow to Havyavaha (fire of the sacred offerings), the eternal one; I worship the Kala of Kavyavaha. (44)

contentBlocks.original:
सूर्याग्निमण्डलां तत्र सकलाद्वादशात्मकम् ।
अर्घ्यपाद्यमहन्तत्र तपिनीं तापिनीं भजे ॥ ४५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sūryāgnimaṇḍalāṃ tatra sakalādvādaśātmakam ।
arghyapādyamahantatra tapinīṃ tāpinīṃ bhajē ॥ 45॥
contentBlocks.translation: Within that I bow to the twelve divine energies (kalas) of the solar disc. I bow to Tapini and Tapini of the arghya (offering-water) and the padya (foot-water). (45)

contentBlocks.original:
धूम्रां मरीचीं वन्देहं ज्वालिनीं मरुहं भजे ।
सुषुम्नाम्भोगदां वन्दे भजे विश्वां च बोधिनीम् ॥ ४६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhūmrāṃ marīchīṃ vandēhaṃ jvālinīṃ maruhaṃ bhajē ।
suṣumnāmbhōgadāṃ vandē bhajē viśvāṃ cha bōdhinīm ॥ 46॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Dhumra and Marichi; I worship Jvalini and Maruha. I bow to Sushumna and the boon-giving one; I bow to Vishva and Bodhini. (46)

contentBlocks.original:
धारिणीं च क्षमां वन्दे सौरीरेताः कलाभजे ।
आश्रयेमण्मलं चान्द्रं तल्कलाषोडशात्मकम् ॥ ४७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhāriṇīṃ cha kṣamāṃ vandē saurīrētāḥ kalābhajē ।
āśrayēmaṇmalaṃ chāndraṃ talkalāṣōḍaśātmakam ॥ 47॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Dharini and Kshama; I worship these Kalas of Sauri. I take refuge in the lunar disc with its sixteen kalas. (47)

contentBlocks.original:
अमृतां मानदां वन्दे पूषां तुष्टीं भजाम्यहम् ।
पुष्टिम्भजे महादेवि भजेऽहं च रतिं धृतिम् ॥ ४८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
amṛtāṃ mānadāṃ vandē pūṣāṃ tuṣṭīṃ bhajāmyaham ।
puṣṭimbhajē mahādēvi bhajē'haṃ cha ratiṃ dhṛtim ॥ 48॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Amrita and Manadha; I worship Pusha and Tushti. I worship Pushti, O Mahadevi, and I worship Rati and Dhriti. (48)

contentBlocks.original:
रशनिं चन्द्रिकां वन्दे कान्तीं जोत्सना श्रियं भजे ।
नेऔमिप्रीतिञ्चागतदाञ्चपूर्ण्णिमाममृताम्भजे ॥ ४९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
raśaniṃ chandrikāṃ vandē kāntīṃ jōtsanā śriyaṃ bhajē ।
nēaumiprītiñchāgatadāñchapūrṇṇimāmamṛtāmbhajē ॥ 49॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Rashani and Chandrika; I worship Kanti and Jyotsna and Shri. I bow to Priti, Agata-da, and the full moon, and I worship the nectar-born. (49)

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिकोणलेखनं कुर्व्वे आकारादिसुरेखकम् ।
हलक्षवर्ण्णसम्युक्तंस्पीतं तं हंसभास्करम् ॥ ५०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trikōṇalēkhanaṃ kurvvē ākārādisurēkhakam ।
halakṣavarṇṇasamyuktaṃspītaṃ taṃ haṃsabhāskaram ॥ 50॥
contentBlocks.translation: I draw the triangle and inscribe within it the letters beginning with Akara — the bright Hamsa and Bhaskara, combined with the vowels and consonants. (50)

contentBlocks.original:
वाक्कामशक्ति संयुक्तं हंसमाराधयाम्यहम् ।
वृत्ताद्बहिःषडश्रस्यलेखनं प्रकरोम्यहम् ॥ ५१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vākkāmaśakti saṃyuktaṃ haṃsamārādhayāmyaham ।
vṛttādbahiḥṣaḍaśrasyalēkhanaṃ prakarōmyaham ॥ 51॥
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the Hamsa united with the Vak-Kama-Shakti. Outside the circle, I draw the hexagon. (51)

contentBlocks.original:
पुरतोग्न्यादिषल्ख़ओणं कखगेनार्च्चयाम्यहम् ।
श्रीविद्ययासप्तवारं करोम्यत्राभि मन्त्रितम् ॥ ५२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
puratōgnyādiṣalkha़ōṇaṃ kakhagēnārchchayāmyaham ।
śrīvidyayāsaptavāraṃ karōmyatrābhi mantritam ॥ 52॥
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the hexagon beginning with Ka-Kha-Ga with the Sri Vidya. With the Sri Vidya I sanctify this seven times. (52)

contentBlocks.original:
समर्प्पयामि देवेशि तस्मात् गन्धाक्षतादिकम् ।
ध्यायामिपूजाद्रव्येषु तत् सर्वं विद्ययायुतम् ॥ ५३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samarppayāmi dēvēśi tasmāt gandhākṣatādikam ।
dhyāyāmipūjādravyēṣu tat sarvaṃ vidyayāyutam ॥ 53॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, I offer to you fragrant powder, flowers and aksharas from that place. I meditate on all the puja materials united with the Vidya. (53)

contentBlocks.original:
चतुर्न्नवतिसन्मन्त्रान् स्पृष्ट्वा तत् प्रजपाम्यहम् ।
वह्नेर्द्दशकलाःसूर्यकलाद्वादशकं भजे ॥ ५४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chaturnnavatisanmantrān spṛṣṭvā tat prajapāmyaham ।
vahnērddaśakalāḥsūryakalādvādaśakaṃ bhajē ॥ 54॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having touched the ninety-four mantras, I recite them here. I worship the ten kalas of Agni (fire) and the twelve kalas of Surya (sun). (54)

contentBlocks.original:
आश्रये शोडषकलास्तत्र चन्द्रमसस्तदा ।
सृष्टिम्वृद्धिं स्मृतिं वन्दे मेधां कान्तीं तथैव च ॥ ५५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āśrayē śōḍaṣakalāstatra chandramasastadā ।
sṛṣṭimvṛddhiṃ smṛtiṃ vandē mēdhāṃ kāntīṃ tathaiva cha ॥ 55॥
contentBlocks.translation: I then take refuge in the sixteen kalas of the moon. I bow to Srishti (creation), Vriddhi (growth), Smriti (memory), Medha (wisdom) and Kanti (radiance). (55)

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्मीं द्युथिं स्थितां वन्दे स्थितिं सिद्धिं भजाम्यहम् ।
एताब्रह्मकलावन्दे जरान्थां पालिनीं भजे ॥ ५६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmīṃ dyuthiṃ sthitāṃ vandē sthitiṃ siddhiṃ bhajāmyaham ।
ētābrahmakalāvandē jarānthāṃ pālinīṃ bhajē ॥ 56॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Lakshmi, Dyuthi and Sthita, and I worship Sthiti and Siddhi. I bow to these Brahma Kalas, and I worship Jara and Palini. (56)

contentBlocks.original:
शान्तिं नमामीश्वरीं च रतीं वन्दे च कारिकाम् ।
वरदांह्लादिनीं वन्दे प्रीतिं दीर्घां भजाभम्यहम् ॥ ५७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śāntiṃ namāmīśvarīṃ cha ratīṃ vandē cha kārikām ।
varadāṃhlādinīṃ vandē prītiṃ dīrghāṃ bhajābhamyaham ॥ 57॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Shanti, I bow to Ishvari, and I bow to Rati and Karika. I bow to Varada and Hladini; I worship Priti, Dirgha and Bhabha. (57)

contentBlocks.original:
एता विष्णुअकलावन्दे तीक्षणां रौद्रिं भयां भजे ।
निद्रान्तन्द्रीं क्षुधां वन्दे नमामि क्रोधिनीं क्रियाम् ॥ ५८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ētā viṣṇuakalāvandē tīkṣaṇāṃ raudriṃ bhayāṃ bhajē ।
nidrāntandrīṃ kṣudhāṃ vandē namāmi krōdhinīṃ kriyām ॥ 58॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to these Vishnu Kalas; I worship Tikshana, Raudri and Bhaya. I bow to Nidra, Tandri and Kshudha; I bow to Krodhini and Kriya. (58)

contentBlocks.original:
उल्कारीं मृत्युरूपां च एता रुद्रकला भजे ।
नीलाम्पीतां भजे श्वेतां वन्देहमरुणां कलाम् ॥ ५९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ulkārīṃ mṛtyurūpāṃ cha ētā rudrakalā bhajē ।
nīlāmpītāṃ bhajē śvētāṃ vandēhamaruṇāṃ kalām ॥ 59॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Ulkari and the death-form Mrityurupa — these are the Rudra Kalas I worship. I worship the Nila, Pita (yellow), Shveta (white), and I bow to the Aruna kala. (59)

contentBlocks.original:
अनन्तख्यां कलाञ्चेति ईश्वरस्य कलाभजे ।
निवृत्तिञ्चप्रतिष्ठाञ्चविद्यांशान्तिं भजाम्यहम् ॥ ६०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anantakhyāṃ kalāñchēti īśvarasya kalābhajē ।
nivṛttiñchapratiṣṭhāñchavidyāṃśāntiṃ bhajāmyaham ॥ 60॥
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the kala named Ananta — these are the kalas of Ishvara. I worship Nivritti, Pratishtha, Vidya and Shanti. (60)

contentBlocks.original:
रोधिकां दीपिकां वन्दे रेचिकां मोचिकां भजे ।
परांसूक्षामृतां सूक्षां प्रणामि कुलेश्वरि! ॥ ६१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rōdhikāṃ dīpikāṃ vandē rēchikāṃ mōchikāṃ bhajē ।
parāṃsūkṣāmṛtāṃ sūkṣāṃ praṇāmi kulēśvari! ॥ 61॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Rodhika and Dipika; I worship Rechika and Mochika. O Kuleshvari, I bow to Para, Sukshma, and Amrita-Sukshma. (61)

contentBlocks.original:
ज्ञानाख्याञ्चनमस्यामि नौमिज्ञानामृतां कलाम् ।
आप्यायिनींव्यापिनीं च मोदिनीं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ ६२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jñānākhyāñchanamasyāmi naumijñānāmṛtāṃ kalām ।
āpyāyinīṃvyāpinīṃ cha mōdinīṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥ 62॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Jnana-named and I bow to the Jnanamrita kala. I bow to Apyayini, Vyapini and Modini. (62)

contentBlocks.original:
कलाः सदाशिवस्यैताः षोडश प्रणमाम्यहम् ।
विष्णुयोनिन्नमस्यामि मूलविद्यां नमाम्यहम् ॥ ६३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalāḥ sadāśivasyaitāḥ ṣōḍaśa praṇamāmyaham ।
viṣṇuyōninnamasyāmi mūlavidyāṃ namāmyaham ॥ 63॥
contentBlocks.translation: These sixteen kalas of Sadashiva — I bow to all of them. I bow to the Vishnu Yoni (source), and I bow to the Mula Vidya. (63)

contentBlocks.original:
त्रैयम्बकं नमस्यामि तद्विष्णुं प्रणमाम्यहम् ।
विष्णुयोनिम्नमस्यामि मूलविद्यां नमाम्यहम् ॥ ६४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
traiyambakaṃ namasyāmi tadviṣṇuṃ praṇamāmyaham ।
viṣṇuyōnimnamasyāmi mūlavidyāṃ namāmyaham ॥ 64॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Tryambaka (three-eyed one) and I bow to Vishnu. I bow to the Vishnu Yoni, and I bow to the Mula Vidya. (64)

contentBlocks.original:
अमृतं मन्त्रितं वन्दे चतुर्न्नवतिभिस्तथा ।
अखण्डैकरसानन्दकरेपरसुधात्मनि ॥ ६५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
amṛtaṃ mantritaṃ vandē chaturnnavatibhistathā ।
akhaṇḍaikarasānandakarēparasudhātmani ॥ 65॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the nectar charged with the ninety-four mantras, in the one who is of the nature of the indivisible, single, essential bliss-consciousness. (65)

contentBlocks.original:
स्वच्छन्दस्पपुरणं मन्त्रं नीधेहि कुलरूपिणि ।
अकुलस्थामृताकारेसिद्धिज्ञानकरेपरे ॥ ६६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
svachChandaspapuraṇaṃ mantraṃ nīdhēhi kularūpiṇi ।
akulasthāmṛtākārēsiddhijñānakarēparē ॥ 66॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Kularupini, place the naturally overflowing mantra-nectar within me. O one who dwells in the akulastha (beyond-kula state), O one of the form of nectar and accomplishment and knowledge. (66)

contentBlocks.original:
अमृतं निधेह्यस्मिन् वस्तुनिक्लिन्नरूपिणि ।
तद्रूपाणेकरस्यत्वङ्कृत्वाह्येतत्स्वरूपिणि ॥ ६७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
amṛtaṃ nidhēhyasmin vastuniklinnarūpiṇi ।
tadrūpāṇēkarasyatvaṅkṛtvāhyētatsvarūpiṇi ॥ 67॥
contentBlocks.translation: O one who is of moist (wet) form, place this nectar in this vessel. Having made this one-essence of that form, O one of that very nature — (67)

contentBlocks.original:
भूत्वा परामृताकारमयि चित् स्पुरणं कुरु ।
एभिर्म्मनूत्तमैर्वन्देमन्त्रितं परमामृतम् ॥ ६८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhūtvā parāmṛtākāramayi chit spuraṇaṃ kuru ।
ēbhirmmanūttamairvandēmantritaṃ paramāmṛtam ॥ 68॥
contentBlocks.translation: — having become the form of supreme nectar, cause the consciousness to flash forth in me. I bow to this supreme nectar charged with these best of mantras. (68)

contentBlocks.original:
जोतिम्मयमिदं वन्दे परमर्घ्यञ्च सुन्दरि ।
तद्विन्दुभिर्मेशिरसि गुरुं सन्तर्प्पयाम्यहम् ॥ ६९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jōtimmayamidaṃ vandē paramarghyañcha sundari ।
tadvindubhirmēśirasi guruṃ santarppayāmyaham ॥ 69॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Sundari, I bow to this luminous supreme arghya (offering-water). With those drops, I gratify the Guru upon my head. (69)

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मास्मिन् तद्विन्दुं कुण्डलिन्यां जुहोम्यहम् ।
हृच्चक्रस्तां-महादेवीम्महात्रिपुरसुन्दरीम् ॥ ७०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmāsmin tadvinduṃ kuṇḍalinyāṃ juhōmyaham ।
hṛchchakrastāṃ-mahādēvīmmahātripurasundarīm ॥ 70॥
contentBlocks.translation: Into the Brahma-fire, I offer those drops into the Kundalini. Into the heart-chakra I meditate on the Goddess — the Mahatripurasundari — (70)

contentBlocks.original:
निरस्तमोहतिमिरां साक्षात् संवित्स्वरूपिणीम् ।
नासापुटात्परकलामथनिर्ग्गमयाम्यहम् ॥ ७१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nirastamōhatimirāṃ sākṣāt saṃvitsvarūpiṇīm ।
nāsāpuṭātparakalāmathanirggamayāmyaham ॥ 71॥
contentBlocks.translation: — who has dispelled the darkness of delusion, who is the direct form of pure consciousness. I bring her forth through the tip of the nose as the supreme kala (divine art). (71)

contentBlocks.original:
नमामियोनिमद्ध्यास्थां त्रिखण्डकुसुमांञ्जलिम् ।
जगन्मातर्महादेवियन्त्रेत्वां स्थापयाम्यहम् ॥ ७२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namāmiyōnimaddhyāsthāṃ trikhaṇḍakusumāṃñjalim ।
jaganmātarmahādēviyantrētvāṃ sthāpayāmyaham ॥ 72॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the one dwelling in the Yoni (sacred source), and I offer the three-sectioned handful of flowers. O mother of the world, O Mahadevi, I install you in the yantra. (72)

contentBlocks.original:
सुधाचैतन्यमूर्त्तीं ते कल्पयामिमनुं तव ।
अनेनदेविमन्त्रयन्त्रेत्वां स्थापयाम्यहम् ॥ ७३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sudhāchaitanyamūrttīṃ tē kalpayāmimanuṃ tava ।
anēnadēvimantrayantrētvāṃ sthāpayāmyaham ॥ 73॥
contentBlocks.translation: I create for you an image of pure consciousness-nectar. With this mantra and yantra, O Goddess, I install you here. (73)

contentBlocks.original:
महापद्मवनान्तस्थे कारणानन्तविग्रहे ।
सर्वभूतहितेमातरेह्यपि परमेश्वरि ॥ ७४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāpadmavanāntasthē kāraṇānantavigrahē ।
sarvabhūtahitēmātarēhyapi paramēśvari ॥ 74॥
contentBlocks.translation: O you who dwell in the great lotus forest, O you of infinite form as the primal cause, O you who are the welfare of all beings, O Mother, O Supreme Goddess — (74)

contentBlocks.original:
देवेशी भक्तसुलभे सर्वाभरणभूषिते ।
यावत्वम्पूजयामीहतावत्त्वं सुस्थिराभव ॥ ७५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvēśī bhaktasulabhē sarvābharaṇabhūṣitē ।
yāvatvampūjayāmīhatāvattvaṃ susthirābhava ॥ 75॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, O easily accessible to devotees, O you adorned with all ornaments — as long as I worship you here, may you remain steady. (75)

contentBlocks.original:
अनेन मन्त्रयुग्मेन त्वामत्रावाहयाम्यहम् ।
कल्पयामिनमः पादमर्घ्यं ते कल्पयाम्यहम् ॥ ७६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anēna mantrayugmēna tvāmatrāvāhayāmyaham ।
kalpayāminamaḥ pādamarghyaṃ tē kalpayāmyaham ॥ 76॥
contentBlocks.translation: With these two mantra-pairs I invoke you here. I offer salutations and I offer arghya (water-offering) to you. (76)

contentBlocks.original:
गन्धतैलाभ्यञ्जनञ्चमज्जशालाप्रवेशम् ।
कल्पयामिनमस्तस्मै मणिपीठोप्रवेशनम् ॥ ७७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gandhatailābhyañjanañchamajjaśālāpravēśam ।
kalpayāminamastasmai maṇipīṭhōpravēśanam ॥ 77॥
contentBlocks.translation: I offer fragrant oil anointing, the bath-hall and its entrance, and the stepping onto the jewelled platform. (77)

contentBlocks.original:
दिव्यस्नानीयमीशानि गृहाणोद्वर्त्तनं शुभे ।
गृहाणोष्णादकस्नानङ्कल्पयाम्यभिषेचनम् ॥ ७८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
divyasnānīyamīśāni gṛhāṇōdvarttanaṃ śubhē ।
gṛhāṇōṣṇādakasnānaṅkalpayāmyabhiṣēchanam ॥ 78॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, O Ishani, accept this divine bath-water. O auspicious one, accept the warm-water bath; I offer the consecration. (78)

contentBlocks.original:
हेमकुम्भायुतैः स्निग्द्धैः कल्पयाम्यभिषेचनम् ।
कल्पयामिनमस्तुभ्यं धेऔतेन परिमार्ज्जनम् ॥ ७९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hēmakumbhāyutaiḥ snigddhaiḥ kalpayāmyabhiṣēchanam ।
kalpayāminamastubhyaṃ dhēautēna parimārjjanam ॥ 79॥
contentBlocks.translation: With golden vessels anointed with fragrant oil, I offer the consecration. I offer salutations and the drying with a white cloth. (79)

contentBlocks.original:
बालभानु प्रतीकाशं दुकूलं परिधानकम् ।
अरुणेनदुकुलेनोत्तरीयं कल्पयाम्यहम् ॥ ८०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bālabhānu pratīkāśaṃ dukūlaṃ paridhānakam ।
aruṇēnadukulēnōttarīyaṃ kalpayāmyaham ॥ 80॥
contentBlocks.translation: I offer a garment radiant as the baby sun, a silken lower garment. I offer the red upper garment. (80)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रवेशनं कल्पयामि सर्वाङ्गानि विलेपनम् ।
नमस्तेकल्पयाम्यत्र मणिपीठोपवेशनम् ॥ ८१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pravēśanaṃ kalpayāmi sarvāṅgāni vilēpanam ।
namastēkalpayāmyatra maṇipīṭhōpavēśanam ॥ 81॥
contentBlocks.translation: I offer the entering of the hall, the anointing of all limbs. I offer salutations and the seating on the jewelled platform. (81)

contentBlocks.original:
अष्टगन्धैः कल्पयामि तवलेखनमम्बिके ।
कालागरुमहाधूपङ्कल्पयामि नमश्शिवे ॥ ८२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṣṭagandhaiḥ kalpayāmi tavalēkhanamambikē ।
kālāgarumahādhūpaṅkalpayāmi namaśśivē ॥ 82॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Ambika, I offer the marks drawn on you with eight fragrant substances. O Shiva, I offer the great incense of black aloe wood (kalaagaru). (82)

contentBlocks.original:
मल्लिकामालातीजाति चम्पकादि मनोरमैः ।
अर्च्चिताङ्कुसुमैर्म्मालां कल्पयामि नमश्शिवे ॥ ८३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mallikāmālātījāti champakādi manōramaiḥ ।
archchitāṅkusumairmmālāṃ kalpayāmi namaśśivē ॥ 83॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Shiva, I offer a garland made of jasmine, tija, champaka and other charming flowers that have been gathered. (83)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रवेशनं कल्पयामि नमो भूषणमण्डपे ।
उपवेश्यंरत्नपीठे तत्रते कल्पयाम्यहम् ॥ ८४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pravēśanaṃ kalpayāmi namō bhūṣaṇamaṇḍapē ।
upavēśyaṃratnapīṭhē tatratē kalpayāmyaham ॥ 84॥
contentBlocks.translation: I offer salutations and the entering of the ornament hall. I offer your being seated on the jewelled platform. (84)

contentBlocks.original:
नवमाणिक्यमकुटं तत्रते कल्पयाम्यहम् ।
शरच्चन्द्रनिभंयुक्तं तच्चन्द्रशकलं तव ॥ ८५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
navamāṇikyamakuṭaṃ tatratē kalpayāmyaham ।
śarachchandranibhaṃyuktaṃ tachchandraśakalaṃ tava ॥ 85॥
contentBlocks.translation: I offer a crown of nine rubies for you there. Upon it is placed the crescent moon that shines like the autumn moon. (85)

contentBlocks.original:
तत सीमन्तसिन्दूरं कस्तूरीतिलकं तव ।
कालाज्ञनङ्कल्पयामि पालीयुगलमुत्तमम् ॥ ८६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tata sīmantasindūraṃ kastūrītilakaṃ tava ।
kālājñanaṅkalpayāmi pālīyugalamuttamam ॥ 86॥
contentBlocks.translation: Then I offer the sindura (vermilion) for the parting of your hair and the kasturi (musk) tilaka (mark). I offer the black collyrium (kala anjana) and the excellent ear-ornaments. (86)

contentBlocks.original:
मणिकुण्डलयुग्मञ्च नासाभरणमीश्वरी! ।
ताटङ्कयुगलन्देवि ललिते धारयाम्यहम् ॥ ८७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
maṇikuṇḍalayugmañcha nāsābharaṇamīśvarī! ।
tāṭaṅkayugalandēvi lalitē dhārayāmyaham ॥ 87॥
contentBlocks.translation: I offer the two gem earrings and the nose ornament, O Goddess. O Lalitha, I adorn you with the two tatanka ear-ornaments. (87)

contentBlocks.original:
अथाद्यां भूषणं कण्ठे महाचिन्ताकमुत्तमम् ।
पदकन्ते कल्पयामि महापदकमुत्तमम् ॥ ८८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
athādyāṃ bhūṣaṇaṃ kaṇṭhē mahāchintākamuttamam ।
padakantē kalpayāmi mahāpadakamuttamam ॥ 88॥
contentBlocks.translation: Now I offer the first ornament for your neck — the excellent great cintamani jewel. Attached to it, I offer the supremely excellent great pendant (padaka). (88)

contentBlocks.original:
मुक्तावलीं कल्पयामि चैकावलि समन्विताम् ।
छन्नवीरञ्चकेयूरयुगलानां चतुष्टयम् ॥ ८९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
muktāvalīṃ kalpayāmi chaikāvali samanvitām ।
Channavīrañchakēyūrayugalānāṃ chatuṣṭayam ॥ 89॥
contentBlocks.translation: I offer the pearl necklace along with the single-strand necklace. I offer four sets of armlets, bangles and rings. (89)

contentBlocks.original:
वलयावलिमालानीं चोर्मिकावलिमीश्वरि ।
काञ्चीदामकटीसूत्रंसौभग्याभरणं च ते ॥ ९०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
valayāvalimālānīṃ chōrmikāvalimīśvari ।
kāñchīdāmakaṭīsūtraṃsaubhagyābharaṇaṃ cha tē ॥ 90॥
contentBlocks.translation: I offer, O Goddess, the rows of bracelets, the rows of rings and the finger-rings, the waist-belt (kanchidama), the hip-cord, and the auspicious ornament. (90)

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिपुरे पादकटकं कल्पये रत्ननूपुरम् ।
पादाङ्गुलीयकन्तुभ्यं पाशमेकं करेतव ॥ ९१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tripurē pādakaṭakaṃ kalpayē ratnanūpuram ।
pādāṅgulīyakantubhyaṃ pāśamēkaṃ karētava ॥ 91॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Tripura, I offer the ankle-bracelets (patakas) and the gem-studded anklets (nupuras). For your hands I offer one noose (pasha). (91)

contentBlocks.original:
अन्ये करेङ्कुशं देवि पूण्ड्रेक्षुधनुषं तव ।
अपरेपुष्पबाणञ्च श्रीमन्माणिक्यपादुके ॥ ९२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anyē karēṅkuśaṃ dēvi pūṇḍrēkṣudhanuṣaṃ tava ।
aparēpuṣpabāṇañcha śrīmanmāṇikyapādukē ॥ 92॥
contentBlocks.translation: In another hand, O Goddess, I offer the goad (ankusha). I offer the sugarcane bow (Pundrekshudhanush) and in yet another hand the flowered arrow. And to your feet I offer the beautiful jewelled sandals. (92)

contentBlocks.original:
तदावरण देवेशि महामञ्चादिरोहणम् ।
कामेश्वराङ्कपर्यङ्कमुपवेशनमुत्तमम् ॥ ९३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tadāvaraṇa dēvēśi mahāmañchādirōhaṇam ।
kāmēśvarāṅkaparyaṅkamupavēśanamuttamam ॥ 93॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, after all these outer enclosure deities I bow to the great throne and the ascending thereof, and the supremely excellent seating on the lap of Kameshvara. (93)

contentBlocks.original:
सुधया पूर्ण्णचषकं ततस्तत् पानमुत्तमम् ।
कर्प्पूरवीटिकान्तुभ्यं कल्पयामि नमः शिवे ॥ ९४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sudhayā pūrṇṇachaṣakaṃ tatastat pānamuttamam ।
karppūravīṭikāntubhyaṃ kalpayāmi namaḥ śivē ॥ 94॥
contentBlocks.translation: I offer the golden vessel filled with nectar, and then the supremely excellent drinking thereof. O Shiva, I offer you the camphor-betel-leaf (karpura-vitika). (94)

contentBlocks.original:
आनन्दोल्लासविलसद्धंसं ते कल्पयाम्यहम् ।
मङ्गलारात्रिकंवन्दे छत्रं ते कल्पयाम्यहम् ॥ ९५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ānandōllāsavilasaddhaṃsaṃ tē kalpayāmyaham ।
maṅgalārātrikaṃvandē Chatraṃ tē kalpayāmyaham ॥ 95॥
contentBlocks.translation: I offer the swan that flutters with blissful joy. I bow to the auspicious waving of the lamps (Mangala-aratrika). I offer you the royal umbrella (chatra). (95)

contentBlocks.original:
चामरं यूगलं देविदर्प्पणं कल्पयाम्यहम् ।
तालव्रिन्तङ्कल्पयामिगन्धपुष्पाक्षतैरपि ॥ ९६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chāmaraṃ yūgalaṃ dēvidarppaṇaṃ kalpayāmyaham ।
tālavrintaṅkalpayāmigandhapuṣpākṣatairapi ॥ 96॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, I offer the two-fold fly-whisk (chamara) and the mirror. I offer the palm-fan (talavinta) and also fragrant flowers and aksharas. (96)

contentBlocks.original:
धूपं दीपश्चनैवेद्यं कल्पयामि शिवप्रिये ।
अथाहम्बैन्दवे चक्रे सर्वानन्दमयात्मके ॥ ९७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhūpaṃ dīpaśchanaivēdyaṃ kalpayāmi śivapriyē ।
athāhambaindavē chakrē sarvānandamayātmakē ॥ 97॥
contentBlocks.translation: I offer incense, lamp, and naivedya (sacred food), O beloved of Shiva. Now, in the Bindu Chakra — which is all-bliss in nature — (97)

contentBlocks.original:
रत्नसिंहासने रम्ये समासीनां शिवप्रियाम् ।
उद्यद्भानुसहस्राभाञ्जपापुष्पसमप्रभाम् ॥ ९८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ratnasiṃhāsanē ramyē samāsīnāṃ śivapriyām ।
udyadbhānusahasrābhāñjapāpuṣpasamaprabhām ॥ 98॥
contentBlocks.translation: — the Goddess who is dear to Shiva, seated on the beautiful gem-throne, radiant like thousands of rising suns, equal in brilliance to the Manipushpa flower — (98)

contentBlocks.original:
नवरत्नप्रभायुक्तमकुटेन विराजिताम् ।
चन्द्ररेखासमोपेताङ्कस्तूरितिलकाङ्किताम् ॥ ९९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
navaratnaprabhāyuktamakuṭēna virājitām ।
chandrarēkhāsamōpētāṅkastūritilakāṅkitām ॥ 99॥
contentBlocks.translation: — crowned with a crown radiant with nine gems, adorned with the crescent moon, marked with a kasturi tilaka — (99)

contentBlocks.original:
कामकोदण्डसौन्दर्यनिर्ज्जितभ्रूलतायुताम् ।
अञ्जनाञ्चितनेत्रान्तुपद्मपत्रनिभेषणाम् ॥ १००॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāmakōdaṇḍasaundaryanirjjitabhrūlatāyutām ।
añjanāñchitanētrāntupadmapatranibhēṣaṇām ॥ 100॥
contentBlocks.translation: — whose arched eyebrows rival a beautiful bow, whose eyes are anointed with collyrium, whose eyes are like the petals of a lotus — (100)

contentBlocks.original:
मणिकुण्डलसम्युक्त कर्ण्णद्वयविराजिताम् ।
ताम्बूलपूरितमुखींसुस्मितास्यविराजिताम् ॥ १०१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
maṇikuṇḍalasamyukta karṇṇadvayavirājitām ।
tāmbūlapūritamukhīṃsusmitāsyavirājitām ॥ 101॥
contentBlocks.translation: — whose two ears are adorned with gem earrings, whose mouth is filled with betel-leaf (tambula), whose face is radiant with a gentle smile — (101)

contentBlocks.original:
आद्यभूषणसम्युक्तां हेमचिन्ताकसंयुताम् ।
पदकेनसमोपेतां महापदकसंयुताम् ॥ १०२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādyabhūṣaṇasamyuktāṃ hēmachintākasaṃyutām ।
padakēnasamōpētāṃ mahāpadakasaṃyutām ॥ 102॥
contentBlocks.translation: — adorned with the first ornament, wearing a golden cintamani, together with a padaka pendant and a great padaka — (102)

contentBlocks.original:
मुक्ताफलसमोपेतामेकावलिसमन्विताम् ।
कौसुभाङ्गदसंयुक्तचतुर्बाहुसमन्विताम् ॥ १०३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
muktāphalasamōpētāmēkāvalisamanvitām ।
kausubhāṅgadasaṃyuktachaturbāhusamanvitām ॥ 103॥
contentBlocks.translation: — adorned with pearl necklace and single-strand necklace, four-armed, wearing armlets together with Kaustubha gem — (103)

contentBlocks.original:
अष्टगन्धसमोपेतां श्रीचन्दनविराजिताम् ।
हेमकुम्भोपमप्रख्यस्तनद्वन्दविराजिताम् ॥ १०४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṣṭagandhasamōpētāṃ śrīchandanavirājitām ।
hēmakumbhōpamaprakhyastanadvandavirājitām ॥ 104॥
contentBlocks.translation: — anointed with eight fragrant substances, resplendent with Sri Chandana sandalwood, whose two breasts are like golden pots — (104)

contentBlocks.original:
रक्तवस्त्रपरीधानां रक्तकञ्चुकसंयुताम् ।
सूक्ष्मरोमावलियुक्ततनुमद्ध्यविराजिताम् ॥ १०५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
raktavastraparīdhānāṃ raktakañchukasaṃyutām ।
sūkṣmarōmāvaliyuktatanumaddhyavirājitām ॥ 105॥
contentBlocks.translation: — wearing a red lower garment and a red bodice, whose slender waist is adorned with the finest hair-line — (105)

contentBlocks.original:
मुक्तामाणिक्यखचित काञ्चीयुतनितम्बनीम् ।
सदाशिवाङकस्थबृहन्महाजघनमण्डलाम् ॥ १०६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
muktāmāṇikyakhachita kāñchīyutanitambanīm ।
sadāśivāṅakasthabṛhanmahājaghanamaṇḍalām ॥ 106॥
contentBlocks.translation: — whose hips are adorned with a pearl-and-ruby waist-belt, whose great, broad hips rest on the lap of Sadashiva — (106)

contentBlocks.original:
कदलिस्तम्भसंराजदूरुद्वयविराजिताम् ।
कपालीकान्तिसङ्काशजङ्घायुगलशोभिताम् ॥ १०७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kadalistambhasaṃrājadūrudvayavirājitām ।
kapālīkāntisaṅkāśajaṅghāyugalaśōbhitām ॥ 107॥
contentBlocks.translation: — whose two thighs are resplendent like plantain stems, whose calves shine with the lustre of the shin-guards of Kapali — (107)

contentBlocks.original:
ग्रूढगुल्फद्वेयोपेतां रक्तपादसमन्विताम् ।
ब्रह्मविष्णुमहेशादिकिरीटस्फूर्ज्जिताङ्घ्रिकाम् ॥ १०८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
grūḍhagulphadvēyōpētāṃ raktapādasamanvitām ।
brahmaviṣṇumahēśādikirīṭasphūrjjitāṅghrikām ॥ 108॥
contentBlocks.translation: — whose two fine ankles are adorned with red feet, whose feet are worshipped by the crowns of Brahma, Vishnu and Maheshvara — (108)

contentBlocks.original:
कान्त्या विराजितपदां भक्तत्राण परायणाम् ।
इक्षुकार्मुकपुष्पेषुपाशाङ्कुशधरांशुभाम् ॥ १०९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāntyā virājitapadāṃ bhaktatrāṇa parāyaṇām ।
ikṣukārmukapuṣpēṣupāśāṅkuśadharāṃśubhām ॥ 109॥
contentBlocks.translation: — whose feet are radiant with divine lustre, who is devoted to protecting devotees, who holds the noose (pasha), the goad (ankusha), the sugarcane bow and the flowered arrow, the beautiful one — (109)

contentBlocks.original:
संवित्स्वरूपिणीं वन्दे ध्यायामि परमेश्वरीम् ।
प्रदर्शयाम्यथशिवेदशामुद्राः फलप्रदाः ॥ ११०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṃvitsvarūpiṇīṃ vandē dhyāyāmi paramēśvarīm ।
pradarśayāmyathaśivēdaśāmudrāḥ phalapradāḥ ॥ 110॥
contentBlocks.translation: — the embodiment of pure consciousness — I bow to and meditate on the Supreme Goddess. I then display to her the ten auspicious mudras (hand-gestures) that bestow results. (110)

contentBlocks.original:
त्वां तर्प्पयामि त्रिपुरे त्रिधना पार्व्वति ।
अग्नौमहेशदिग्भागे नैरृत्र्यां मारुते तथा ॥ १११॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvāṃ tarppayāmi tripurē tridhanā pārvvati ।
agnaumahēśadigbhāgē nairṛtryāṃ mārutē tathā ॥ 111॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Tripura, I gratify you with the three offerings. In the fire corner (southeast), in the Maheshvara direction (northeast), in the Nairiti (southwest), and in the Maruta (northwest) direction — (111)

contentBlocks.original:
इन्द्राशावारुणी भागे षडङ्गान्यर्च्चये क्रमात् ।
आद्याङ्कामेश्वरीं वन्दे नमामि भगमालिनीम् ॥ ११२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
indrāśāvāruṇī bhāgē ṣaḍaṅgānyarchchayē kramāt ।
ādyāṅkāmēśvarīṃ vandē namāmi bhagamālinīm ॥ 112॥
contentBlocks.translation: — in the Indra direction (east) and the Varuna (west) direction, I worship the six limbs in order. I bow to the primal Kameshvari; I bow to Bhagamalini. (112)

contentBlocks.original:
नित्यक्लिन्नां नमस्यामि भेरुण्डां प्रणमाम्यहम् ।
वह्निवासान्नमस्यामि महाविद्येश्वरीं भजे ॥ ११३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nityaklinnāṃ namasyāmi bhēruṇḍāṃ praṇamāmyaham ।
vahnivāsānnamasyāmi mahāvidyēśvarīṃ bhajē ॥ 113॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Nityaklinna; I bow to Bherunda. I bow to Vahnivasini; I worship Mahavidyeshvari. (113)

contentBlocks.original:
शिवदूतिं नमस्यामि त्वरितां कुल सुन्दरीम् ।
नित्यान्नीलपताकाञ्च विजयां सर्वमङ्गलाम् ॥ ११४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śivadūtiṃ namasyāmi tvaritāṃ kula sundarīm ।
nityānnīlapatākāñcha vijayāṃ sarvamaṅgalām ॥ 114॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Shivaduti; I bow to Tvarita and Kula Sundari. I bow to Nitya, Nila-Pataka, Vijaya and Sarva Mangala. (114)

contentBlocks.original:
ज्वालामालाञ्च चित्राञ्च महानित्यां च संस्तुवे ।
प्रकाशानन्दनाथाख्याम्पराशक्तिनमाम्यहम् ॥ ११५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jvālāmālāñcha chitrāñcha mahānityāṃ cha saṃstuvē ।
prakāśānandanāthākhyāmparāśaktinamāmyaham ॥ 115॥
contentBlocks.translation: I praise Jvala-Mala, Chitra and Maha-Nitya. I bow to the Parashakti known as Prakashananda Natha. (115)

contentBlocks.original:
शुक्लदेवीं नमस्यामि प्रणमामि कुलेश्वरीम् ।
परशिवानन्दनाथाख्याम्पराशक्ति नमाम्यहम् ॥ ११६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śukladēvīṃ namasyāmi praṇamāmi kulēśvarīm ।
paraśivānandanāthākhyāmparāśakti namāmyaham ॥ 116॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the white goddess; I bow to Kuleshvari. I bow to the Parashakti known as Parashivananda Natha. (116)

contentBlocks.original:
कौलेश्वरानन्दनाथं नौमि कामेश्वरीं सदा ।
भोगानन्दन्नमस्यामि सिद्धौघञ्च वरानने ॥ ११७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaulēśvarānandanāthaṃ naumi kāmēśvarīṃ sadā ।
bhōgānandannamasyāmi siddhaughañcha varānanē ॥ 117॥
contentBlocks.translation: I always bow to the Kauleshvarananda Natha and to Kameshvari. I bow to Bhogananda; I bow to the group of Siddhas, O fair-faced one. (117)

contentBlocks.original:
क्लिन्नानन्दं नमस्यामि समयानन्दमेवच ।
सहजानन्दनाथञ्चप्रणमामि मुहुर्मुहु ॥ ११८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
klinnānandaṃ namasyāmi samayānandamēvacha ।
sahajānandanāthañchapraṇamāmi muhurmuhu ॥ 118॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Klinnnananda; I bow to Samayananda. I bow again and again to Sahajananda Natha. (118)

contentBlocks.original:
मानवौघं नमस्यामि गगनानन्दगप्यहम् ।
विश्वानन्दन्नमस्यामि विमलानन्दमेवच ॥ ११९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mānavaughaṃ namasyāmi gaganānandagapyaham ।
viśvānandannamasyāmi vimalānandamēvacha ॥ 119॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Manava lineage (mana-vaugah); I bow to Gagananda. I bow to Vishvananda; I bow to Vimalananda. (119)

contentBlocks.original:
मदनानन्दनाथञ्च भुवनानन्दरूपिणीम् ।
लीलानन्दन्नमस्यामि स्वात्मानन्दं महेश्वरि ॥ १२०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
madanānandanāthañcha bhuvanānandarūpiṇīm ।
līlānandannamasyāmi svātmānandaṃ mahēśvari ॥ 120॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Madanananda Natha and to the one of the nature of Bhuvananda. I bow to Lilananda; I bow to Svatmananda, O Maheshvari. (120)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रणमामिप्रियानन्दं सर्वकामफलप्रदम् ।
परमेष्टिगुरुंवन्दे परमङ्गुरुमाश्रये ॥ १२१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
praṇamāmipriyānandaṃ sarvakāmaphalapradam ।
paramēṣṭiguruṃvandē paramaṅgurumāśrayē ॥ 121॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Priyananda, the bestower of all desired fruits. I bow to the Parameshti Guru; I take refuge in the Parama Guru. (121)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीगुरुं प्रणमस्यामि मूर्द्ध्नि ब्रह्मबिलेश्वरीम् ।
श्रीमदानन्दनाथाख्यश्रिगुरोपादुकां तथा ॥ १२२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīguruṃ praṇamasyāmi mūrddhni brahmabilēśvarīm ।
śrīmadānandanāthākhyaśrigurōpādukāṃ tathā ॥ 122॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Sri Guru; on my crown I bow to Brahmavileshvari. I also bow to the sandals of the Sri Guru known as Shriman Ananda Natha. (122)

contentBlocks.original:
अथ प्राथमिके देवि चतुरश्रे कुलेश्वरि ।
अणिमांलखिमां वन्दे महिमां प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ १२३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha prāthamikē dēvi chaturaśrē kulēśvari ।
aṇimāṃlakhimāṃ vandē mahimāṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥ 123॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess, now in the first square (outer enclosure) I bow to Anima and Laghima, O Kuleshvari. I bow to Mahima. (123)

contentBlocks.original:
ईशित्वसिद्धिं कलये वशित्वं प्रणमाम्यहम् ।
प्राकाम्यसिद्धिम्भुक्तिञ्च इच्छाप्राप्र्तिमहं भजे ॥ १२४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īśitvasiddhiṃ kalayē vaśitvaṃ praṇamāmyaham ।
prākāmyasiddhimbhuktiñcha ichChāprāprtimahaṃ bhajē ॥ 124॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Ishitva Siddhi and Vashitva; I worship Prakamya, Bhukti (enjoyment) and Ichchha Prapti. (124)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वकामप्रदां सर्वकामसिद्धिमहं भजे ।
मद्ध्यमेचतुरश्रेहं ब्राह्मीं माहेश्वरीं भजे ॥ १२५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvakāmapradāṃ sarvakāmasiddhimahaṃ bhajē ।
maddhyamēchaturaśrēhaṃ brāhmīṃ māhēśvarīṃ bhajē ॥ 125॥
contentBlocks.translation: I worship Sarvakama Prada (bestower of all desires) and the Sarvakama Siddhi. In the middle square, I bow to Brahmi and Maheshvari. (125)

contentBlocks.original:
कौमारीं वैष्णवीं वन्दे वाराहीं प्रणमाम्यहम् ।
माहेन्द्रीमपिचामुण्डाम्महालक्ष्मीमहं भजे ॥ १२६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaumārīṃ vaiṣṇavīṃ vandē vārāhīṃ praṇamāmyaham ।
māhēndrīmapichāmuṇḍāmmahālakṣmīmahaṃ bhajē ॥ 126॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Kaumari and Vaishnavi; I bow to Varahi. I bow to Mahendri and also Chamunda; I worship Mahalakshmi. (126)

contentBlocks.original:
तृतीये चतुरश्रे तु सर्वसङ्क्षोभिणीं भजे ।
सर्वविद्रापिणीम्मुद्रां सर्वाकर्षिणिकां भजे ॥ १२७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tṛtīyē chaturaśrē tu sarvasaṅkṣōbhiṇīṃ bhajē ।
sarvavidrāpiṇīmmudrāṃ sarvākarṣiṇikāṃ bhajē ॥ 127॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the third square, I worship Sarva-Sankshobhini (the agitator of all). I worship Sarvavidravini (the disperser of all) and Sarvakarshini (the attractor of all). (127)

contentBlocks.original:
मुद्रां वशङ्करीं वन्दे सर्वोन्मादिनिकां भजे ।
भजेमहाङ्कुशां मुद्रां खेचरीं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ १२८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mudrāṃ vaśaṅkarīṃ vandē sarvōnmādinikāṃ bhajē ।
bhajēmahāṅkuśāṃ mudrāṃ khēcharīṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥ 128॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Vasankari mudra; I worship Sarvaonmadini (the maddener of all). I worship the Mahankusha mudra; I bow to Khechari. (128)

contentBlocks.original:
बीजामुद्रां योनिमुद्रां भजे सर्वत्रिखण्डिनीम् ।
त्रैलोक्यमोहनञ्चक्रं नमामि ललिते तव ॥ १२९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bījāmudrāṃ yōnimudrāṃ bhajē sarvatrikhaṇḍinīm ।
trailōkyamōhanañchakraṃ namāmi lalitē tava ॥ 129॥
contentBlocks.translation: I worship the Bija mudra and the Yoni mudra; I worship Sarva-Trikhandini. I bow to your Trailokya Mohana Chakra (the chakra that enchants the three worlds), O Lalitha. (129)

contentBlocks.original:
नमामि योगिनीं तत्र प्रखटाख्यामभीष्टदाम् ।
सुधार्ण्णवासनंवन्दे तत्र ते परमेश्वरि ॥ १३०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namāmi yōginīṃ tatra prakhaṭākhyāmabhīṣṭadām ।
sudhārṇṇavāsanaṃvandē tatra tē paramēśvari ॥ 130॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Yogini therein named Prakata, who bestows all desired objects. I bow to your supreme throne there, O Goddess. (130)

contentBlocks.original:
चक्रेश्वरि महं वन्दे त्रिपुरां प्रणमाम्यहम् ।
सर्वसङ्क्षोभिणीम्मुद्रां ततोहं कलये शिवे ॥ १३१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chakrēśvari mahaṃ vandē tripurāṃ praṇamāmyaham ।
sarvasaṅkṣōbhiṇīmmudrāṃ tatōhaṃ kalayē śivē ॥ 131॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Chakreshvari; I bow to Tripura. I then contemplate the Sarva-Sankshobhini mudra, O Shiva. (131)

contentBlocks.original:
अथाहं षोडशदले कामाकर्षिणिकां भजे ।
बुद्ध्याकर्षिणिकां वन्देऽहङ्काराकर्षिणीं भजे ॥ १३२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
athāhaṃ ṣōḍaśadalē kāmākarṣiṇikāṃ bhajē ।
buddhyākarṣiṇikāṃ vandē'haṅkārākarṣiṇīṃ bhajē ॥ 132॥
contentBlocks.translation: Now in the sixteen-petalled lotus I worship Kamakarshinika (attracting desire). I bow to Buddhyakarshinika (attracting intelligence) and Ahankara akarshinika (attracting ego). (132)

contentBlocks.original:
शब्दाकर्षिणिकां वन्दे स्पर्शाकर्षिणिकां भजे ।
रूपाकर्षिणिकांवन्दे रसाकर्षिणिकां भजे ॥ १३३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śabdākarṣiṇikāṃ vandē sparśākarṣiṇikāṃ bhajē ।
rūpākarṣiṇikāṃvandē rasākarṣiṇikāṃ bhajē ॥ 133॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Shabdakarshinika (attracting sound); I worship Sparshakarshinika (attracting touch). I bow to Rupakarshinika (attracting form); I worship Rasakarshinika (attracting taste). (133)

contentBlocks.original:
गन्धाकर्षिणिकां वन्दे चित्ताकर्षिणिकां भजे ।
धैर्याकर्षिणिकांवन्दे स्मृत्याकर्षिणिकां भजे ॥ १३४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gandhākarṣiṇikāṃ vandē chittākarṣiṇikāṃ bhajē ।
dhairyākarṣiṇikāṃvandē smṛtyākarṣiṇikāṃ bhajē ॥ 134॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Gandhakarshinika (attracting smell); I worship Chittakarshinika (attracting mind). I bow to Dhairyakarshinika (attracting courage); I worship Smrityakarshinika (attracting memory). (134)

contentBlocks.original:
नामाकर्षिणिकां वन्दे बीजाकर्षिणिकां भजे ।
आत्माकर्षिणिकांवन्दे अमृताकर्षिणिकां भजे ॥ १३५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nāmākarṣiṇikāṃ vandē bījākarṣiṇikāṃ bhajē ।
ātmākarṣiṇikāṃvandē amṛtākarṣiṇikāṃ bhajē ॥ 135॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Namakarshinika (attracting name); I worship Bijakarshinika (attracting seed-mantra). I bow to Atmakarshinika (attracting the Self); I worship Amritakarshinika (attracting nectar). (135)

contentBlocks.original:
शरीराकर्षिणिकां वन्दे नित्यां श्रीपरमेश्वरि ।
सर्वाशापूरकंवन्दे कल्पयेहं तवेश्वरि ॥ १३६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śarīrākarṣiṇikāṃ vandē nityāṃ śrīparamēśvari ।
sarvāśāpūrakaṃvandē kalpayēhaṃ tavēśvari ॥ 136॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Sharirakarshinika (attracting the body), the eternal one, O Sri Parameshvari. I bow to Sarvashapuraka (fulfiller of all hopes), O Goddess, I offer it to you. (136)

contentBlocks.original:
गुप्ताख्यां योगिनीं वन्दे मातरं गुप्तपूज्यताम् ।
पोताम्बुजासनन्तत्र नमामि ललिते तव ॥ १३७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
guptākhyāṃ yōginīṃ vandē mātaraṃ guptapūjyatām ।
pōtāmbujāsanantatra namāmi lalitē tava ॥ 137॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Yogini there named Gupta (the hidden one), the secretly worshipped mother. I bow to the lotus-seat of gold there, O Lalitha. (137)

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिपुरेशीं नमस्यामि भजामिष्टार्त्थसिद्धिदाम् ।
सर्वविद्राविणिमुद्रान्तत्राहं ते विचन्तये ॥ १३८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tripurēśīṃ namasyāmi bhajāmiṣṭārtthasiddhidām ।
sarvavidrāviṇimudrāntatrāhaṃ tē vichantayē ॥ 138॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to TripureshI; I worship the bestower of all desired objects. I contemplate for you, O Shiva, the Sarvavidravini mudra. (138)

contentBlocks.original:
सिवे तवाष्टपत्रेहमनङ्गकुसुमां भजे ।
अनङ्गमेखलांवन्दे अनङ्गमदनां भजे ॥ १३९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sivē tavāṣṭapatrēhamanaṅgakusumāṃ bhajē ।
anaṅgamēkhalāṃvandē anaṅgamadanāṃ bhajē ॥ 139॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Shiva, in your eight-petalled lotus I worship Anangakusuma. I bow to Anangamekhala; I worship Anangamadana. (139)

contentBlocks.original:
नमोहं प्रणस्यामि अनङ्गमदनातुराम् ।
अनङ्गरेखाङ्कलये भजेनङ्गां च वेगिनीम् ॥ १४०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namōhaṃ praṇasyāmi anaṅgamadanāturām ।
anaṅgarēkhāṅkalayē bhajēnaṅgāṃ cha vēginīm ॥ 140॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow and I bow to Anangamadanatura. I contemplate Anangarekha; I worship Ananga and Vegini. (140)

contentBlocks.original:
अनङ्गाकुशवन्देऽहमनङ्गमालिनीं भजे ।
तत्राहम्प्रणस्यामि देव्या आसनमुत्तमम् ॥ १४१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anaṅgākuśavandē'hamanaṅgamālinīṃ bhajē ।
tatrāhampraṇasyāmi dēvyā āsanamuttamam ॥ 141॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Anangakusha; I worship Anangamalini. I bow to the supreme seat of the Goddess there. (141)

contentBlocks.original:
नमामि जगतीशानीं तत्र त्रिपुरसुन्दरीम् ।
सर्वाकर्षिणिकाम्मुद्रां तत्राह कल्पयामिते ॥ १४२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namāmi jagatīśānīṃ tatra tripurasundarīm ।
sarvākarṣiṇikāmmudrāṃ tatrāha kalpayāmitē ॥ 142॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Jagatishani (ruler of the universe) there — the Tripurasundari. I contemplate for you the Sarvakarshini mudra there. (142)

contentBlocks.original:
भुवनाश्रये तव शिवे सर्वसङ्क्षोभिणीं भजे ।
सर्वविद्राविणींवन्दे सर्वकर्षिणिकां भजे ॥ १४३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhuvanāśrayē tava śivē sarvasaṅkṣōbhiṇīṃ bhajē ।
sarvavidrāviṇīṃvandē sarvakarṣiṇikāṃ bhajē ॥ 143॥
contentBlocks.translation: In your enclosure of worlds, O Shiva, I worship Sarva-Sankshobhini. I bow to Sarvavidravini; I worship Sarvakarshini. (143)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वह्लादिनीं वन्दे सर्वसम्मोहिनीं भजे ।
सकलस्तम्भिनीं वन्दे कलये सर्वजृम्भिणीम् ॥ १४४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvahlādinīṃ vandē sarvasammōhinīṃ bhajē ।
sakalastambhinīṃ vandē kalayē sarvajṛmbhiṇīm ॥ 144॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Sarvahladini; I worship Sarvasammohini. I bow to Sarvastambhini (the one who paralyses all); I contemplate Sarvajrimbhini (the one who makes all yawn/expand). (144)

contentBlocks.original:
वशङ्करीं नमस्यामि सर्वरज्ञिनिकां भजे ।
सकलोन्मदिनीं वन्दे भजे सर्वार्थसाधके ॥ १४५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vaśaṅkarīṃ namasyāmi sarvarajñinikāṃ bhajē ।
sakalōnmadinīṃ vandē bhajē sarvārthasādhakē ॥ 145॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Vashankari; I worship Sarvaranjinika. I bow to Sarvalalonmadini; I worship Sarvaarthasadhaka (accomplisher of all objects). (145)

contentBlocks.original:
सम्पत्तिपुरिकां वन्दे सर्वमन्त्रमयीं भजे ।
भजाम्येवततश्शक्तिं सर्वद्वन्द्वक्ष्यङ्करीम् ॥ १४६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sampattipurikāṃ vandē sarvamantramayīṃ bhajē ।
bhajāmyēvatataśśaktiṃ sarvadvandvakṣyaṅkarīm ॥ 146॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Sampattihurika; I worship Sarvamantramayimahi. I also worship the Shakti that destroys all pairs of opposites (dvandvas). (146)

contentBlocks.original:
तत्राहं कलये चक्रं सर्वसौभाग्यदायकम् ।
नमामिजगतां धात्रीं सम्प्रदायाख्ययोगिनिम् ॥ १४७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tatrāhaṃ kalayē chakraṃ sarvasaubhāgyadāyakam ।
namāmijagatāṃ dhātrīṃ sampradāyākhyayōginim ॥ 147॥
contentBlocks.translation: I contemplate there the Chakra of Sarvasaubhagyadayaka (bestower of all good fortune). I bow to the Yogini there named Sampradaya, the sustainer of the world. (147)

contentBlocks.original:
नमामि परमेशानीं महात्रिपुरवासिनिम् ।
कलयेहन्तव शिवे मुद्रां सर्वशङ्करीम् ॥ १४८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namāmi paramēśānīṃ mahātripuravāsinim ।
kalayēhantava śivē mudrāṃ sarvaśaṅkarīm ॥ 148॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the supreme Goddess, the dweller in the great Tripura. O Shiva, I contemplate for you the Sarva-Shankari mudra. (148)

contentBlocks.original:
बहिर्द्दशारे ते देवि सर्वसिद्धिप्रदां भजे ।
सर्वसम्पत्प्रदां वन्दे सर्वप्रियङ्करीं भजे ॥ १४९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bahirddaśārē tē dēvi sarvasiddhipradāṃ bhajē ।
sarvasampatpradāṃ vandē sarvapriyaṅkarīṃ bhajē ॥ 149॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the outer ten-angle chakra, O Goddess, I worship Sarvasiddhiprada (bestower of all accomplishments). I bow to Sarvasampat-prada (bestower of all wealth); I worship Sarvapriyankari (one who pleases all). (149)

contentBlocks.original:
नमाम्यहं ततो देवीं सर्वमङ्गलकारिणीम् ।
सर्वकामप्रदांवन्दे सर्वदुःखविमोचिनिम् ॥ १५०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namāmyahaṃ tatō dēvīṃ sarvamaṅgalakāriṇīm ।
sarvakāmapradāṃvandē sarvaduḥkhavimōchinim ॥ 150॥
contentBlocks.translation: I then bow to the Goddess Sarvamangalakarini (maker of all auspiciousness). I bow to Sarvakama-prada (bestower of all desires); I bow to Sarvadukha-vimochini (liberator from all sorrows). (150)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वमृत्युप्रशमनीं सर्वविघ्ननिवारिणीम् ।
सर्वाङ्गसुन्दरींवन्दे सर्वसौभाग्यदायिनीम् ॥ १५१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvamṛtyupraśamanīṃ sarvavighnanivāriṇīm ।
sarvāṅgasundarīṃvandē sarvasaubhāgyadāyinīm ॥ 151॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Sarvamrityuprashamani (pacifier of all death); I bow to Sarvavigananivarini (remover of all obstacles). I bow to Sarvangsundari (beautiful in all limbs); I bow to Sarvasaubhagyadayini (bestower of all good fortune). (151)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वार्त्थसाधकं चक्रं तत्राहं ने विचिन्तये ।
तत्राहन्ते नमस्यामि कुलोत्तीर्णाख्य योगिनीम् ॥ १५२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvārtthasādhakaṃ chakraṃ tatrāhaṃ nē vichintayē ।
tatrāhantē namasyāmi kulōttīrṇākhya yōginīm ॥ 152॥
contentBlocks.translation: I contemplate there the Sarvaarthasadhaka Chakra. I bow there to the Yogini known as Kulottirna. (152)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वमन्त्रसनं वन्दे त्रिपुराश्रियमाश्रये ।
कलयामिततो मुद्रां सर्वोन्मादन कारिणीम् ॥ १५३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvamantrasanaṃ vandē tripurāśriyamāśrayē ।
kalayāmitatō mudrāṃ sarvōnmādana kāriṇīm ॥ 153॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Sarvamantra-seat; I take refuge in Tripurashri. I then contemplate the mudra of Sarvaonmadanakari (maker of all ecstasy). (153)

contentBlocks.original:
अन्तर्द्दशारे ते देवि सर्वज्ञां प्रणमाम्यहम् ।
सर्वशक्तिन्नमस्यामि सर्वैश्वर्यप्रदां भजे ॥ १५४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
antarddaśārē tē dēvi sarvajñāṃ praṇamāmyaham ।
sarvaśaktinnamasyāmi sarvaiśvaryapradāṃ bhajē ॥ 154॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the inner ten-angle chakra, O Goddess, I bow to Sarvajña (all-knowing). I bow to Sarva-Shakti (all power); I worship the bestower of all sovereignty. (154)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वज्ञानमयीं वन्दे सर्वव्याधिविनाशिनीम् ।
सर्वाधारस्वरूपाञ्चसर्वपापहराम्भजे ॥ १५५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvajñānamayīṃ vandē sarvavyādhivināśinīm ।
sarvādhārasvarūpāñchasarvapāpaharāmbhajē ॥ 155॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Sarvajnanamayim (embodiment of all knowledge); I bow to Sarvavyadhi-vinashini (destroyer of all disease). I bow to Sarvadhara-svarupa (form of all supports); I worship Sarvapapa-hara (remover of all sins). (155)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वानन्दमयिं वन्दे सर्वरक्षास्वरूपिणीम् ।
प्रणमामिमहादेवीं सर्वेप्सित फलप्रदाम् ॥ १५६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvānandamayiṃ vandē sarvarakṣāsvarūpiṇīm ।
praṇamāmimahādēvīṃ sarvēpsita phalapradām ॥ 156॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Sarvanandamayim (embodiment of all bliss); I bow to Sarvaraksha-svarupini (of the form of all protection). I bow to the Mahadevi; I bow to the bestower of all desired fruits. (156)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वरक्षाकरं चक्रं सुन्दरीं कलये सदा ।
निगर्भयोनींवन्दे तत्राहं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ १५७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvarakṣākaraṃ chakraṃ sundarīṃ kalayē sadā ।
nigarbhayōnīṃvandē tatrāhaṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥ 157॥
contentBlocks.translation: I always contemplate the Sarvarakshakara Chakra and the Sundari. I bow there to the Nigarbhayoni (the hidden womb). (157)

contentBlocks.original:
साद्ध्यसिद्धासनं वन्दे भजे त्रिपुरमालिनीम् ।
कलयामिततो देवीं मुद्रां सर्वमहाङ्कुशाम् ॥ १५८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sāddhyasiddhāsanaṃ vandē bhajē tripuramālinīm ।
kalayāmitatō dēvīṃ mudrāṃ sarvamahāṅkuśām ॥ 158॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Sadhyasiddhasana (the seat of the perfected accomplished); I worship the Tripura Malini. I then contemplate the Goddess — the mudra of Sarva Mahankusha. (158)

contentBlocks.original:
अष्टारे वशिनीं वन्दे महा कामेश्वरीं भजे ।
मोदिनींविमलांवन्दे अरुणाजयिनीं भजे ॥ १५९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṣṭārē vaśinīṃ vandē mahā kāmēśvarīṃ bhajē ।
mōdinīṃvimalāṃvandē aruṇājayinīṃ bhajē ॥ 159॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the eight-angle chakra I bow to Vasini; I worship the Maha Kameshvari. I bow to Modini and Vimala; I bow to Aruna and Jayini. (159)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वेश्वरीं नमस्यामि कौलिनीं प्रणमाम्यहम् ।
सर्वरोगहरञ्चक्रं तत्राहं कलये सदा ॥ १६०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvēśvarīṃ namasyāmi kaulinīṃ praṇamāmyaham ।
sarvarōgaharañchakraṃ tatrāhaṃ kalayē sadā ॥ 160॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Sarveshvari; I bow to Kaulini. I always contemplate there the Sarvarogahara Chakra (chakra that removes all diseases). (160)

contentBlocks.original:
नमामि त्रिपुरा सिद्धिं भजे मुद्रां च खेचरीम् ।
महात्रिकोणवत्बाहुचतुरश्रे कुलेश्वरि ॥ १६१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namāmi tripurā siddhiṃ bhajē mudrāṃ cha khēcharīm ।
mahātrikōṇavatbāhuchaturaśrē kulēśvari ॥ 161॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Tripura Siddhi; I bow to the Khechari mudra. O Kuleshvari — in the great triangle-shaped arm and in the square: (161)

contentBlocks.original:
नमामि जृम्भणाबाणं सर्वसम्मोहिनीं भजे ।
पाशञ्चापं भजे नित्यं भजे स्तम्भनमङ्कुशम् ॥ १६२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namāmi jṛmbhaṇābāṇaṃ sarvasammōhinīṃ bhajē ।
pāśañchāpaṃ bhajē nityaṃ bhajē stambhanamaṅkuśam ॥ 162॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Jrimbhanabana (arrow of yawning/expansion) and to the Sarvasammohini. I worship the noose and the bow; I always worship the goad and the paralysing (stambhana). (162)

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिकोणेहं जगद्धात्रीं महाकामेश्वरीं भजे ।
महावज्रेश्वरींवन्दे महाश्रीभगमालिनीम् ॥ १६३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trikōṇēhaṃ jagaddhātrīṃ mahākāmēśvarīṃ bhajē ।
mahāvajrēśvarīṃvandē mahāśrībhagamālinīm ॥ 163॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the triangle I bow to Jagaddhatri (sustainer of the world) and to Maha Kameshvari. I bow to Maha Vajreshvari; I bow to the Maha Shri Bhagamalini. (163)

contentBlocks.original:
महाश्रीसुन्दरीं वन्दे सर्वकामफलप्रदाम् ।
सर्वसिद्धिप्रदञ्चक्रं तवदेवि नमाम्यहम् ॥ १६४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāśrīsundarīṃ vandē sarvakāmaphalapradām ।
sarvasiddhipradañchakraṃ tavadēvi namāmyaham ॥ 164॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Maha-Shri-Sundari, the bestower of all desired fruits. O Goddess, I bow to the Sarvasiddhiprada Chakra (the chakra bestowing all accomplishments). (164)

contentBlocks.original:
नमाम्यतिरहस्याख्यां योगिनीं तवकामदाम् ।
त्रिपुराम्बान्नमस्यामि बीजामुद्रामहाम्भजे ॥ १६५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namāmyatirahasyākhyāṃ yōginīṃ tavakāmadām ।
tripurāmbānnamasyāmi bījāmudrāmahāmbhajē ॥ 165॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the supremely secret Yogini — the Atirahasy-akhya — who grants all desires. I bow to Tripuramba; I bow to the Bija mudra and the Mahamba. (165)

contentBlocks.original:
मूलमन्त्रेण ललिते तल्बिन्दौ पूजयाम्यहम् ।
सर्वानन्दमयञ्चक्रं तवदेवि भजाम्यहम् ॥ १६६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mūlamantrēṇa lalitē talbindau pūjayāmyaham ।
sarvānandamayañchakraṃ tavadēvi bhajāmyaham ॥ 166॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lalitha, with the root mantra I worship you in the Bindu (central point). O Goddess, I worship the Sarvanandam Chakra (chakra of all bliss). (166)

contentBlocks.original:
परां पररहस्याख्यां योगिनीं तत्रकामदाम् ।
महाचक्रेश्वरींवन्दे योनिमुद्रामहं भजे ॥ १६७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
parāṃ pararahasyākhyāṃ yōginīṃ tatrakāmadām ।
mahāchakrēśvarīṃvandē yōnimudrāmahaṃ bhajē ॥ 167॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the supreme Para-Rahasya Yogini therein who grants all desires. I bow to the Maha Chakreshvari; I bow to the Yoni mudra. (167)

contentBlocks.original:
धूपदीपादिकं सर्वमर्प्पितं कल्पयाम्यहम् ।
त्वल्प्रीतयेमहामुद्रां दर्शयामि ततश्शिवे ॥ १६८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhūpadīpādikaṃ sarvamarppitaṃ kalpayāmyaham ।
tvalprītayēmahāmudrāṃ darśayāmi tataśśivē ॥ 168॥
contentBlocks.translation: I offer incense, lamps and all else that has been placed. O Shiva, I then display to you the Maha Mudra (the great gesture). (168)

contentBlocks.original:
शाल्यन्नं मधुसम्युक्तं पायसापूप सम्युक्तम् ।
घृतसूपसमायुक्तन्दधिक्षीरसमन्वितम् ॥ १६९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śālyannaṃ madhusamyuktaṃ pāyasāpūpa samyuktam ।
ghṛtasūpasamāyuktandadhikṣīrasamanvitam ॥ 169॥
contentBlocks.translation: I offer to you cooked rice with honey, payasa (sweet rice), fried cakes, clarified butter and lentil soup, curd and milk — (169)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वभक्ष्यसमायुक्तं बहुशाकसमन्वितम् ।
निक्षिप्यकाञ्चने पात्रे नैवेद्यं कल्पयामि ते ॥ १७०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvabhakṣyasamāyuktaṃ bahuśākasamanvitam ।
nikṣipyakāñchanē pātrē naivēdyaṃ kalpayāmi tē ॥ 170॥
contentBlocks.translation: — together with all eatables and many kinds of greens. Placing all this in a golden vessel, I offer the naivedya (sacred food) to you. (170)

contentBlocks.original:
सङ्कल्पबिन्दुना चक्रं कुचौ बिन्दुद्वयेन च ।
योनिश्चसपरार्द्धेन कृत्वा श्रीललिते तव ॥ १७१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saṅkalpabindunā chakraṃ kuchau bindudvayēna cha ।
yōniśchasaparārddhēna kṛtvā śrīlalitē tava ॥ 171॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Lalitha, with the sankalpabindu (consciousness-dot) in the chakra, with two dots on the breasts, and with the yoni in the upper half — I have formed your Kamakala (the sacred geometric form). (171)

contentBlocks.original:
एतत् कामकला रूपं भक्तानां सर्वकामदम् ।
सर्वसौभाग्यदंवन्दे तत्र त्रिपुरसुन्दरीम् ॥ १७२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ētat kāmakalā rūpaṃ bhaktānāṃ sarvakāmadam ।
sarvasaubhāgyadaṃvandē tatra tripurasundarīm ॥ 172॥
contentBlocks.translation: This Kamakala form bestows all desires on devotees. I bow to the Tripurasundari therein, the bestower of all good fortune. (172)

contentBlocks.original:
वामभागे महेशानि वृत्तं च चतुरस्रकम् ।
कृत्वागन्धाक्षताद्यैश्चाप्यर्च्चयामि महेश्वरीम् ॥ १७३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vāmabhāgē mahēśāni vṛttaṃ cha chaturasrakam ।
kṛtvāgandhākṣatādyaiśchāpyarchchayāmi mahēśvarīm ॥ 173॥
contentBlocks.translation: On your left side, O Maheshani, I draw a circle and a square. With fragrant powder, flowers and aksharas, I worship the Maheshvari. (173)

contentBlocks.original:
वाग्दवाद्यं नमस्यामि तत्र व्यापकमण्डलम् ।
जलयुक्तेनपाणौ च शुद्धमुद्रा समन्वितम् ॥ १७४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vāgdavādyaṃ namasyāmi tatra vyāpakamaṇḍalam ।
jalayuktēnapāṇau cha śuddhamudrā samanvitam ॥ 174॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to the Vagdeva (goddess of speech) and to the pervading circle. With water-filled hands and the pure mudra — (174)

contentBlocks.original:
तत्र मन्त्रेण दास्यामि देवि ते बलिमुत्तमम् ।
नमस्तेदेवदेवेशि नम स्त्रैलोक्यवन्दिते ॥ १७५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tatra mantrēṇa dāsyāmi dēvi tē balimuttamam ।
namastēdēvadēvēśi nama strailōkyavanditē ॥ 175॥
contentBlocks.translation: — with that mantra, O Goddess, I offer you the supreme bali (offering). Salutations to you, O God of gods, O God! Salutations, O one worshipped by the three worlds! (175)

contentBlocks.original:
नमश्शिववराङ्कस्थे नमस्त्रीपुरसुन्दरि ।
प्रदक्षिणनमस्कारमनेनाहं करोमि ते ॥ १७६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namaśśivavarāṅkasthē namastrīpurasundari ।
pradakṣiṇanamaskāramanēnāhaṃ karōmi tē ॥ 176॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to you, O seated on the lap of Shiva! Salutations, O Tripurasundari! I now offer to you this pradakshina (circumambulation) and namaskar. (176)

contentBlocks.original:
तत सङ्कल्पमन्त्राणां समाजं परमेश्वरि ।
प्रजपामिमहाविद्यां त्वत् प्रीत्यर्त्थमहं शिवे ॥ १७७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tata saṅkalpamantrāṇāṃ samājaṃ paramēśvari ।
prajapāmimahāvidyāṃ tvat prītyartthamahaṃ śivē ॥ 177॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Supreme Goddess, I then recite the assembled collection of Kalpa mantras. O Shiva, I recite the Mahavidya for your gratification. (177)

contentBlocks.original:
तव विद्यां प्रजप्त्वाथ नौमि त्वां परमेश्वरि ।
महादेविमहेशानि महाशिवमये प्रिये ॥ १७८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tava vidyāṃ prajaptvātha naumi tvāṃ paramēśvari ।
mahādēvimahēśāni mahāśivamayē priyē ॥ 178॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having recited your Vidya, I now praise you, O Supreme Goddess. O Mahadevi, O Maheshani, O one composed of Maha Shiva, O beloved — (178)

contentBlocks.original:
महानित्ये महासिद्धे त्वामहं शरणं शिवे ।
जयत्वन्त्रिपुरे देवि ललिते परमेश्वरि ॥ १७९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahānityē mahāsiddhē tvāmahaṃ śaraṇaṃ śivē ।
jayatvantripurē dēvi lalitē paramēśvari ॥ 179॥
contentBlocks.translation: O eternal one, O great accomplished one — O Shiva, I take refuge in you. Victory to you, O Tripura, O Lalitha, O Supreme Goddess. (179)

contentBlocks.original:
सदाशिव प्रियङ्करि पाहिमां करुणानिधे ।
जगन्मातर्ज्जगद्रूपेजगदीश्वरवल्लभे ॥ १८०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sadāśiva priyaṅkari pāhimāṃ karuṇānidhē ।
jaganmātarjjagadrūpējagadīśvaravallabhē ॥ 180॥
contentBlocks.translation: O one dear to Sadashiva, O treasury of compassion — protect me! O mother of the universe, O form of the universe, O beloved of the lord of the universe — (180)

contentBlocks.original:
जगन्मयि जगत् स्तुत्ये गौरि त्वामहमाश्रये ।
अनाद्येसर्वलोकानामाद्ये भक्तेष्टदायिनि ॥ १८१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jaganmayi jagat stutyē gauri tvāmahamāśrayē ।
anādyēsarvalōkānāmādyē bhaktēṣṭadāyini ॥ 181॥
contentBlocks.translation: O one who pervades the universe, O one worthy of the world's praise, O Gauri — I take refuge in you. O beginningless one, O primal one of all worlds, O bestower of all desired objects to devotees — (181)

contentBlocks.original:
गिरिराजेन्द्रतनये नमस्तीपुरसुन्दरि ।
जयारीञ्जयदेवेशिब्रह्ममातर्महेश्वरि ॥ १८२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
girirājēndratanayē namastīpurasundari ।
jayārīñjayadēvēśibrahmamātarmahēśvari ॥ 182॥
contentBlocks.translation: O daughter of the mountain-king, salutations to you, O Tripurasundari! Victory, O conqueror of enemies! Victory, O sovereign Goddess! O Brahma's mother, O Maheshvari! (182)

contentBlocks.original:
विष्णुमातरमाद्यन्ते हरमातस्सुरेश्वरि ।
ब्रह्म्यादिमातृसंस्तुत्ये सर्वाभरण सम्युक्ते ॥ १८३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viṣṇumātaramādyantē haramātassurēśvari ।
brahmyādimātṛsaṃstutyē sarvābharaṇa samyuktē ॥ 183॥
contentBlocks.translation: O mother of Vishnu, O primordial and eternal one, O Hara's mother, O Sureshvari! O one praised by the mothers headed by Brahmi, O one adorned with all ornaments! (183)

contentBlocks.original:
ज्योतिर्मयि महारूपे पाहिमां त्रिपुरे सदा ।
लक्ष्मीवाण्यादिसं पूज्ये ब्रह्मविष्णुशिवप्रिय ॥ १८४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jyōtirmayi mahārūpē pāhimāṃ tripurē sadā ।
lakṣmīvāṇyādisaṃ pūjyē brahmaviṣṇuśivapriya ॥ 184॥
contentBlocks.translation: O one of luminous form, O one of great form, O Tripura — protect me always! O one worshipped by Lakshmi, Vani and others, O beloved of Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva! (184)

contentBlocks.original:
भजामि तव पादाब्जं देवि त्रिपुरसुन्दरि ।
त्वल्प्रीत्यर्त्थंयतः काञ्चीच्छक्तिं वैपूजयाम्यहम् ॥ १८५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhajāmi tava pādābjaṃ dēvi tripurasundari ।
tvalprītyartthaṃyataḥ kāñchīchChaktiṃ vaipūjayāmyaham ॥ 185॥
contentBlocks.translation: I worship your lotus feet, O Goddess, O Tripurasundari! For your gratification, I worship the Shakti of Kanchi (the golden girdle). (185)

contentBlocks.original:
ततश्च केतनां शक्तिं तर्पयामि महेश्वरि ।
तथापित्वां भजंस्तोषं चिदग्नौ च ददाम्यहम् ॥ १८६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tataścha kētanāṃ śaktiṃ tarpayāmi mahēśvari ।
tathāpitvāṃ bhajaṃstōṣaṃ chidagnau cha dadāmyaham ॥ 186॥
contentBlocks.translation: Then I gratify the banner Shakti, O Maheshvari. Even so, I offer everything into the fire of consciousness, worshipping you. (186)

contentBlocks.original:
त्वल्प्रीत्यर्थ्यं महादेवि ममाभीष्टार्त्थ सिद्धये ।
बद्ध्वात्वां खैचरीमुद्रां क्षमस्वोद्वासयाम्यहम् ॥ १८७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvalprītyarthyaṃ mahādēvi mamābhīṣṭārttha siddhayē ।
baddhvātvāṃ khaicharīmudrāṃ kṣamasvōdvāsayāmyaham ॥ 187॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Mahadevi, for your gratification and for the fulfilment of my desired objects — having bound you with the Khechari mudra, I now forgive myself and perform the udvasa (farewell). (187)

contentBlocks.original:
तिष्ठमे हृदयेनित्यं त्रिपुरे परमेश्वरि ।
जगदम्बमहाराज्ञि महाशक्ति शिवप्रिये ॥ १८८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tiṣṭhamē hṛdayēnityaṃ tripurē paramēśvari ।
jagadambamahārājñi mahāśakti śivapriyē ॥ 188॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Tripura, O Supreme Goddess — dwell in my heart always. O mother of the universe, O great queen, O great power, O beloved of Shiva — (188)

contentBlocks.original:
हृच्चक्रे तिष्तमे नित्यं महात्रिपुरसुन्दरि ।
एतत्त्रिपुरसुन्दर्या हृदयं सर्वकामदम् ॥ १८९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hṛchchakrē tiṣtamē nityaṃ mahātripurasundari ।
ētattripurasundaryā hṛdayaṃ sarvakāmadam ॥ 189॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Mahatripurasundari — dwell in my heart-chakra always. This Hridayam of Tripurasundari bestows all desired objects. (189)

contentBlocks.original:
महारहस्यं सततं दुर्ल्लभं दैवतैरपि ।
साक्षात्सदाशिवेनोक्तं गुह्यात् गुह्यमनुत्तमम् ॥ १९०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahārahasyaṃ satataṃ durllabhaṃ daivatairapi ।
sākṣātsadāśivēnōktaṃ guhyāt guhyamanuttamam ॥ 190॥
contentBlocks.translation: It is the greatest secret, eternally difficult to attain even by the gods. It was directly declared by Sadashiva himself — supremely secret among all secrets. (190)

contentBlocks.original:
यः पतेत् श्रद्धया नित्यं श‍ऋणुयाद्वा समाहितः ।
नित्यपूजाफलन्देव्यास्सलभेन्नात्र संशयः ॥ १९१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yaḥ patēt śraddhayā nityaṃ śa‍ṛṇuyādvā samāhitaḥ ।
nityapūjāphalandēvyāssalabhēnnātra saṃśayaḥ ॥ 191॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever reads this daily with faith, or hears it with an attentive mind — shall without doubt attain the fruits of the daily worship of the Goddess. (191)

contentBlocks.original:
पापैः समुच्यते सद्यः कायवाक्क् सित्तसम्भवैः ।
पूर्वजन्मसमुत् भ्रदतैर्ज्ञानाज्ञकृतैरपि ॥ १९२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pāpaiḥ samuchyatē sadyaḥ kāyavākk sittasambhavaiḥ ।
pūrvajanmasamut bhradatairjñānājñakṛtairapi ॥ 192॥
contentBlocks.translation: One is immediately freed from sins committed through body, speech and mind, whether they arose in previous births or in this life, whether done knowingly or unknowingly. (192)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वक्रतुषुयत् पुण्यं सर्वतीर्त्थेषु यर्फलम् ।
तत्पुण्यं लभते नित्यं मानवो नात्र संशयः ॥ १९३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvakratuṣuyat puṇyaṃ sarvatīrtthēṣu yarphalam ।
tatpuṇyaṃ labhatē nityaṃ mānavō nātra saṃśayaḥ ॥ 193॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whatever merit comes from all Vedic sacrifices, whatever fruit comes from visiting all sacred pilgrimage sites — that merit is attained daily by this person without any doubt. (193)

contentBlocks.original:
अचलां लभते लक्ष्मीं त्रैलोक्येनाति दुर्लभाम् ।
साक्षाद्विष्णुर्महालक्ष्याशीघ्रमेव भविष्यति ॥ १९४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
achalāṃ labhatē lakṣmīṃ trailōkyēnāti durlabhām ।
sākṣādviṣṇurmahālakṣyāśīghramēva bhaviṣyati ॥ 194॥
contentBlocks.translation: One attains the unshakeable Lakshmi that is supremely rare in all three worlds. Very soon, Vishnu himself shall come in the company of Mahalakshmi. (194)

contentBlocks.original:
अष्टैश्वर्य मवाप्नोति स शीघ्रं मानवोत्तमः ।
घण्डिकापादुकासिद्ध्यादिष्टकंशीघ्रमश्नुते ॥ १९५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṣṭaiśvarya mavāpnōti sa śīghraṃ mānavōttamaḥ ।
ghaṇḍikāpādukāsiddhyādiṣṭakaṃśīghramaśnutē ॥ 195॥
contentBlocks.translation: That best of persons quickly attains the eight forms of sovereignty. He quickly attains the accomplishment of the ganda (bell), sandals (paduka), and other desired eight things. (195)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीमत्त्रिपुराम्बिकायै नमः ।
॥ श्रीललिताहृदयस्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīmattripurāmbikāyai namaḥ ।
॥ śrīlalitāhṛdayastōtraṃ sampūrṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations to the blessed Tripurambika (Lalitha). Thus ends the Sri Lalitha Hridaya Stotram completely.

contentBlocks.original: ॐ तत् सत् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: ōṃ tat sat ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Om Tat Sat.
